runners.name: Wolfpack
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m on good ground at Thirsk last summer for James Ferguson; poor form in juvenile hurdles; a race of this type represents his best chance of adding to his tally, judged on his recent Dundalk fourth behind a stablemate of Tyson Fury.
runners.comment: 1m winner in Britain, recent AW fourth behind a stablemate of Tyson Fury was encouraging

runners.name: Tyson Fury
runners.spotlight: Takes a sharp drop in class having contested a strong 1m6f handicap at the Curragh last week; had previously shown decent form in finishing second in maiden hurdles at Tramore and Killarney; suited by the race conditions, as he was when readily landing a race of this type at Dundalk in March.
runners.comment: Suited by the race conditions as he was when an easy winner in this grade on AW in March

runners.name: Merlin The Wizard
runners.spotlight: Three wins in Britain in 2023 at up to 1m; regressive in the last two seasons; produced best run for some time at Dundalk in March when good second over 1m; more recent turf form just about good enough to suggest he can make his presence felt in this grade.
runners.comment: Recent form just about good enough to suggest he can make his presence felt at this level

runners.name: Dreamed To Dream
runners.spotlight: Rating of 64 flatters him on what he has shown in five Flat starts; tailed off at Cork on latest on his first start for a fourth different trainer.
runners.comment: Rating of 64 flatters him on what he has shown in five Flat starts, tailed off last time

runners.name: Zammawar
runners.spotlight: Has struggled in maidens, including a Ballinrobe event in which Glendower finished third.
runners.comment: Has struggled in maidens, including a Ballinrobe event in which Glendower finished third

runners.name: Glendower
runners.spotlight: Placed four times in Britain in 2024, showing a liking for quick ground; modest form over hurdles for this stable last year; after almost 12 months off, produced a much livelier display when third in a Flat maiden at Ballinrobe last month.
runners.comment: Gave a lively display after a break when third in a maiden at Ballinrobe last month

runners.name: Taj Crown
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and ran green at Dundalk on her only start at three; made late progress in a 1m maiden at Killarney last month; unexposed filly, open to improvement but others have stronger credentials.
runners.comment: Unexposed filly, open to improvement but others have stronger credentials

runners.name: Timandi
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden; never in the first three; unlikely to improve her profile here.
runners.comment: 13-race maiden, never in the first three. unlikely to improve her profile here.
runners.owner: Mrs N K Griffin & T Brosnan

runners.name: Kakori
runners.spotlight: Filly with a good German pedigree; ran her best race so far when sixth in a 20-runner handicap over 1m5f at Navan in April, beaten only 3l and would have been closer with a clear run; several others have a stronger chance in theory, but perhaps unwise to rule out.
runners.comment: Good pedigree, others have a stronger chance in theory but perhaps unwise to rule out

runners.name: Peace Walk
runners.spotlight: Failed to prosper in handicaps after showing decent form at two; had dropped from an initial rating of 85 to 70 by the time of his final start for Charlie Johnston in 2024; gelded now; likely to need after a long absence.
runners.comment: Failed to prosper in handicaps after showing decent form at two, long absence to overcome

runners.name: Chestnut Palace
runners.spotlight: Just okay efforts in two handicaps, not looking to stay 1m4f at Leopardstown and again didn't get home under a positive ride over 1m2f at Down Royal; cheekpieces now added but obvious stamina concerns.
runners.comment: Cheekpieces now added but obvious stamina concerns

runners.name: Servare
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on seasonal return at Navan in March (1m2f, heavy); not so good in 1m4f events since, upset in stalls at Cork and made much of running in first-time cheekpieces at Leopardstown before fading; headgear left off now but remains unproven at this trip; drops in grade.
runners.comment: Navan 1m2f winner hasn't convinced at this trip since

runners.name: Celestially
runners.spotlight: Navan fourth (to Servare) on handicap debut was a step in the right direction but never sighted at Killarney behind Femme Beauty; handicapper relenting but more needed and cheekpieces tried.
runners.comment: Good bit to find on recent form; cheekpieces tried

runners.name: Blissful Bonita
runners.spotlight: Didn't count but not beaten far on last month's handicap debut (behind Femme Beauty) at Killarney over slightly shorter; cheekpieces now added so don't rule out.
runners.comment: Not beaten far on last month's handicap debut at Killarney; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Delusional
runners.spotlight: Recent 1m3f Killarney handicap (first-time visor) third to Femme Beauty a definite step in the right direction; one to consider from a yard hitting form.
runners.comment: Killarney third one to consider from a yard hitting form

runners.name: Femme Beauty
runners.spotlight: Got a good ride from the front on speed-favouring track when winning over 1m3f at Killarney last month; didn't seem to get home over 1m4f at Roscommon six days later; 10lb claimer back aboard here.
runners.comment: Killarney winner didn't stay 1m4f at Roscommon 6 days later; Foley deserts

runners.name: Glen Echo
runners.spotlight: No show when well held in three decent maidens in the spring; seems the lesser likely of the Cromwell pair on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Seems the lesser likely of the Cromwell pair on handicap debut

runners.name: Cascade Canyon
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutant whose three maiden runs have been at 1m2f; potential improver upped in trip but opposable off this opening mark.
runners.comment: Potential improver upped in trip but opposable off this opening mark

runners.name: Lord Aus
runners.spotlight: Eye-catching handicap debut at Down Royal last month when strong-finishing third third (Chestnut Palace 2.75l away fourth) over 1m2f, still looking green; longer trip seems sure to suit and cheekpieces added; big player.
runners.comment: Strong-finishing Down Royal third should relish this longer trip; big player

runners.name: Retro Gal
runners.spotlight: Never competitive in three maidens over 1m; half-sister to 1m2f AW winner and is out of a Nathaniel mare, so good chance this trip will suit; market has to be watched on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Never competitive in three maidens over 1m; bred to improve for this step up in trip

runners.name: The Holy Apostle
runners.spotlight: Standout 2yo effort when placed in Killarney nursery; quite exposed and will need to improve for this step up in trip on seasonal debut.
runners.comment: 0-7 at two; will need to improve for this step up in trip on seasonal debut

runners.name: Amethyst Stone
runners.spotlight: Hasn't counted in three maidens over middle-distances but likely improver upped in trip on handicap debut; yard in top form.
runners.comment: Likely improver upped in trip on handicap debut; yard in top form

runners.name: Point Cartwright
runners.spotlight: Late headway on handicap debut over 1m2f at Ballinrobe last month suggests this longer trip may suit; one to consider.
runners.comment: Ballinrobe run suggests this longer trip may suit; one to consider

runners.name: Leeward
runners.spotlight: Well-bred son of Frankel hasn't made an impact in three maidens over shorter but could improve for this step up in trip on handicap debut.
runners.comment: No impact in maidens over shorter, could improve for this step up in trip on handicap bow

runners.name: Kirkland Sioux
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation filly has failed to make an impact in three maidens over shorter; upped markedly in trip for handicap debut and can't be ruled out (dam won over this trip).
runners.comment: Upped markedly in trip for handicap debut and can't be ruled out (dam won over this trip)

runners.name: Royal Sapphire
runners.spotlight: Maiden is 0-7 over shorter trips, three times this year; could improve for the step up in trip but others look more likely.
runners.comment: 0-7; could improve for the step up in trip but others look more likely

runners.name: Res Ipsa
runners.spotlight: Frankel brother to yard's smart Group 3 winner Red Letter; well fancied on sole 2yo outing but looked raw and met trouble in running; reappeared with tongue-tie fitted for much improved effort last month at Killarney, clear with odds-on winner over this trip; looks the one to beat.
runners.comment: Clear Killarney second last month makes him the one to beat

runners.name: Henry Hudson
runners.spotlight: Only mild promise in two maidens last month, likely more one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Only mild promise in two maidens last month, likely more one for handicaps

runners.name: Camino Lad
runners.spotlight: Flintshire gelding cost 25,000euros as a foal; first foal; dam French 1m4f winner (inc Listed; RPR 107), half-sister to French 1m7f Group 2 winner Ponte Tresa; debutant appears the yard second-string.
runners.comment: Debutant appears the yard second-string

runners.name: Pimlico Piranha
runners.spotlight: Saxon Warrior colt is first foal of a 1m3f/12.3f winner (RPR 98), a half-sister to winners Choreography (7f 2yo) and Ardenlee Star (6f-1m inc 2yo); debutant probably best watched.
runners.comment: Debutant probably best watched

runners.name: Willie's Mill
runners.spotlight: Profitable gelding is first foal of an unraced half-sister to winners Goldinthesea (1m), Shawaaheq (7.4f-8.5f) and Qawamees (1m4f); best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Profitable gelding best watched on debut

runners.name: Stable Lane
runners.spotlight: Shaped okay in both 1m4f Dundalk maiden runs late last year; down markedly in trip for turf debut and eligible for a mark after this.
runners.comment: Both AW runs over 1m4f; eligible for a mark after this

runners.name: I Carry You Home
runners.spotlight: Too Darn Hot colt cost 70,000gns as a yearling; half-brother to five winners inc Law Power (7f-1m2f inc 2yo/Italian Listed), Perfect Clarity (1m 2yo/11.6f Listed) and Clarentine (1m AW); dam Listed-placed 7f 2yo winner (stayed 1m2f); McMonagle's pick from yard trio so debutant has to be respected.
runners.comment: McMonagle's pick from yard trio so debutant has to be respected

runners.name: Poet's Willow
runners.spotlight: Triple-figure odds for all four starts and ran accordingly.
runners.comment: Triple-figure odds for all four starts and ran accordingly

runners.name: Roman Sands
runners.spotlight: Big prices when down the field in recent maidens at Gowran and Fairyhouse.
runners.comment: Big prices when down the field in recent maidens at Gowran and Fairyhouse

runners.name: Six Wings
runners.spotlight: Awaiting a handicap mark after being soundly beaten in all three outings in May.
runners.comment: Awaiting a handicap mark after being soundly beaten in all three outings in May

runners.name: Highwayman
runners.spotlight: Well held but excuses in both recent maiden runs, slowly away at Cork and hampered at the Curragh a week ago; eligible for a mark after this.
runners.comment: Well held but excuses in both recent maiden runs; eligible for a mark after this

runners.name: Footstepstoglory
runners.spotlight: Placed hurdler never sighted on Flat debut at Gowran 9 days ago; best watched.
runners.comment: Placed hurdler never sighted on Flat debut at Gowran 9 days ago; best watched

runners.name: Shay's Dream
runners.spotlight: New Bay colt cost 36,000euros as a yearling and 35,000euros at the breeze-ups; half-brother to winners Double Effect (French 10.5f/1m4f Flat and 2m1f hurdle) and Shangri La (Spanish 6f-1m2f inc 2yo); dam French 6f 2yo winner (RPR 96); tailed off on AW debut in November; gelded since and best watched on turf debut.
runners.comment: Tailed off on AW debut in November; gelded since and best watched on turf debut

runners.name: Flame Of Forest
runners.spotlight: Fair form at 7f/1m in Britain; appeared not to stay this trip at Newcastle in January; same remark could apply to his Irish debut at the Curragh.
runners.comment: Probably best around at this trip, poor over 1m2f on AW in Britain and on Irish debut

runners.name: Royal Saxo
runners.spotlight: Seldom ran a bad race when trained in France; notching five wins and ten places from 20 starts; at his most effective over a bit further than this on AW, but won over 7f on soft early in his career; could be a useful recruit for Tony Martin
runners.comment: Seldom ran a bad race when trained in France and could be a useful recruit for Tony Martin

runners.name: Monvoe
runners.spotlight: Twice runner-up in nurseries on AW last autumn; reappeared for back-to-back win over C&D and when running away late over the same trip at Leopardstown; second over an extra furlong at the same venue last week; at the right end of the handicap with a good 7lb claimer aboard.
runners.comment: At the right end of the handicap with an attractive profile and a good 7lb claimer

runners.name: Dragon Of Malta
runners.spotlight: Has recorded three of his seven wins at Listowel; poor effort there ten days ago, when last of 13; also failed to beat a single rival at Cork on first outing of the season.
runners.comment: Has recorded three of his seven wins at Listowel, poor effort there ten days ago

runners.name: Earls
runners.spotlight: Confirmed a liking for the Roscommon track with last month's second; last September, that venue was the scene of the most recent of his 11 career wins; retains ability.
runners.comment: Confirmed his liking for the Roscommon track with last month's second, retains ability

runners.name: Calm The Jets
runners.spotlight: Landed a 1m maiden at Dundalk on debut early last year; did not appear last turf season; has finished in mid-division on all three starts in turf handicaps; needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: AW winner on debut, has finished in mid-division on all three starts in turf handicaps

runners.name: Highbury See See
runners.spotlight: Both wins last year came on soft and heavy ground; fair fourth on good at Roscommon on penultimate start; uneven form this season; others preferred.
runners.comment: Both wins came last season on soft ground, uneven form this season, others preferred

runners.name: Noli Timere
runners.spotlight: Won two races over this trip last August; final two starts in Listed races were ambitious; never in the hunt on reappearance in the Irish Lincoln; sights lowered now.
runners.comment: Never in the hunt on reappearance in the Irish Lincoln, sights lowered now. trip suits

runners.name: Pierre Royal
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner over 1m at Gowran for Dermot Weld on the second of three starts last season; unplaced favourite for a 1m1f Leopardstown event almost exactly 12 months ago on his only handicap start.
runners.comment: Maiden winner for Dermot Weld, unplaced favourite 12 months ago on his only handicap start

runners.name: Ohmali
runners.spotlight: Heavy-ground winner over this trip at Gowran last September and handled quicker ground well at Leopardstown last month when coming from good bit back to finish second to an improver (third won next time); one to consider, despite wide draw.
runners.comment: Leopardstown second augurs well; one to consider, despite wide draw

runners.name: Bucaneer's Spirit
runners.spotlight: Four wins at around this trip, most recently off 60 at Cork in April; decent third at Roscommon on latest so remains on a competitive mark; one to consider.
runners.comment: Decent third at Roscommon on latest so remains on a competitive mark; one to consider

runners.name: Usuario Amigo
runners.spotlight: 7f Brighton maiden win in September 2024; yet to score for this yard, best effort when third in a rated race here last summer (6.5f, good), testing ground probably wasn't ideal at Cork last time.
runners.comment: Ran well in rated race here last summer, prospects if back to that form

runners.name: Perfect Judgement
runners.spotlight: Four AW wins, sole turf win came at Leopardstown (7f); didn't count on both runs last month for current yard under 7lb claimer but badly hampered by loose horse second occasion; senior rider takes over and don't rule out off career-low mark.
runners.comment: Excuses last time; senior rider takes over and don't rule out off career-low mark

runners.name: Hexagonal
runners.spotlight: Maiden with a little enough mileage on the clock; creditable efforts in handicaps of late but more needed.
runners.comment: Creditable efforts in handicaps of late but more needed

runners.name: Mythical Rock
runners.spotlight: First reserve; now with his fifth different trainer; gained an overdue breakthrough win at Dundalk in January, two turf runs since for this yard okay; not one to readily rule out.
runners.comment: AW winner; two turf runs since for this yard okay, not one to readily rule out; reserve

runners.name: Jasmine Affanalis
runners.spotlight: Tipperary winner last summer was only just denied a second career win over this trip at Killarney last month; 2lb higher but major player.
runners.comment: Just denied a second career win over this trip at Killarney last month;  major player

runners.name: Java Wood
runners.spotlight: 40-1 win on AW/handicap debut last August and solid opening efforts on turf this season, including Leopardstown fourth (Ohmali second) over this trip; didn't count at the Curragh on latest but Keane booking takes the eye.
runners.comment: Didn't count at the Curragh on latest but Keane booking takes the eye

runners.name: Friars Gate
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; lightly raced maiden whose recent handicap debut run was hampered by a slow start; new headgear combination fitted.
runners.comment: Handicap debut was hampered by slow start; headgear combination fitted; reserve

runners.name: Out On Friday
runners.spotlight: Eight career wins have been over mid-distances, most recently nicely improved from comeback run when second at Fairyhouse last month (1m2f) after setting a decent pace; back in trip here.
runners.comment: Wins all over further; recent Fairyhouse second augurs well if managing this drop in trip

runners.name: Mags
runners.spotlight: Sole win came on AW in late 2024; back from long absence when well held over C&D (first-time hood) in April and has changed yards again since.
runners.comment: Well held over C&D (first-time hood) in April and has changed yards again since

runners.name: Robot Rock
runners.spotlight: Placed on AW in France; slowly away, soundly beaten both starts last month for this yard; big drop in trip and wide draw here.
runners.comment: Slowly away, soundly beaten both starts last month; big drop in trip and wide draw

runners.name: Notforalongtime
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; prolific AW winner is just 1-29 on turf but some good placings this season at Cork and the Curragh; remains of interest off lower turf mark.
runners.comment: Prolific AW winner remains of interest off lower turf mark; reserve

runners.name: Goofy Gander
runners.spotlight: Never counted after slow start on handicap debut at the Curragh in April (6f); half-sister to 1m4f Group 3 winner Trustyourinstinct so can be expected to improve as she goes up in trip; market has to be watched.
runners.comment: Expected improver at this trip and beyond; watch market

runners.name: Genuine Jim
runners.spotlight: Both wins on soft, including over this trip at Cork last season; two runs this season poor and ideally wants it softer so opposable.
runners.comment: Two runs this season poor and ideally wants it softer so opposable

runners.name: Imnotleavinyou
runners.spotlight: Placed in a Naas nursery last term and runner-up in her first three outings this season at 6f/7f/1m, each time staying on late; ground may have been too quick for her when she failed to reproduce her best form at Down Royal 12 days ago; on balance, appeals more than Beau Army, fourth in that event.
runners.comment: Second in her first three races this term at 6f/7f/1m, may bounce back from below-par run

runners.name: Lorr's Girl
runners.spotlight: Best form at Cork, the scene of her only win (over 7f in September 2024) and last season's best effort, in a 6f contest; failed to make much impact on seasonal debut at Killarney.
runners.comment: Best run last year was over 6f at Cork, no impact over 1m on seasonal debut at Killarney

runners.name: Carvalhal
runners.spotlight: Winner over 1m1f on quick ground in Britain; placed three times in a row last June when headgear went on; form tailed off in the autumn; will need more racing to get back to top form on the evidence of last month's reappearance at Gowran.
runners.comment: Placed three times in a row last June, may need more racing to get back to top form

runners.name: Bright Image
runners.spotlight: Produced a fine effort when fifth of 20 over 6f at the Curragh at the beginning of the 2024 season; absent until returning at Gowran three weeks ago; posed no threat, not helped by a slip early in the races; best watched.
runners.comment: Started 2024 season with a good run, did not appear again until last month, best watched

runners.name: James Henry
runners.spotlight: 2-17 on AW, 0-15 on turf; did not appear again last year after finishing third of 19 in a 1m apprentice handicap at Leopardstown in May; after a year off, plenty to like about his stable debut at Listowel ten days ago, ran on to take third ahead of today's rival Linger For Longer.
runners.comment: Plenty to like about his stable debut at Listowel, slight edge over Linger For Longer

runners.name: Oxford Circus
runners.spotlight: Good effort when third in a 6f maiden on debut at Dundalk in February; has failed to build on that; soundly beaten in four turf handicap stars at four different trips from 5f to 1m.
runners.comment: Placed on AW, soundly beaten in four turf handicap stars at different trips from 5f to 1m

runners.name: Shelbourne Flyer
runners.spotlight: Maiden after 13 starts; a few good 7f turf runs last summer, notably when second over this trip at Leopardstown; first run for Noel Meade now.
runners.comment: 13-race maiden, managed a few good 7f turf runs last summer, first start for Noel Meade

runners.name: Beau Army
runners.spotlight: Has won over 6f/7f at Dundalk and runner-up over 1m at the AW venue in April; ran his best race on turf so far when keeping on for fourth behind Meridien (a winner again since) over 7f at Down Royal 12 days ago; among those behind then was Imnotleavinyou whose previous form gives her a leading chance here.
runners.comment: Two AW wins, best turf rum so far when fourth at Down Royal with Imnotleavinyou behind

runners.name: Jazzy Dancer
runners.spotlight: No win on turf since 2022 but scored at Dundalk (7f) for the third time in February; not at his best at the same venue the following month; better when seventh of 18 at Fairyhouse recently, closely matched with Verhoyen on that running
runners.comment: No win on turf since 2022 but scored at Dundalk for the third time in February

runners.name: Racing Royalty
runners.spotlight: Both wins came in the autumn of 2023; best 2024 form was also late in the campaign, and his only placing last season was obtained at Galway in September; hard to make a case for him on seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Has typically shown his best form late in the season, may take a while to hit a peak again

runners.name: She's On The Ball
runners.spotlight: Weak form in maidens; towards the rear at Cork on handicap debut; 3lb out of the handicap; no positives apart from the booking of Wayne Lordan.
runners.comment: Weak form, 3lb out of the handicap, no positives apart from the booking of Wayne Lordan

runners.name: Verhoyen
runners.spotlight: Eight-time winner (placed 20 times) who marked his 100th appearance with a fair sixth of 18 at Fairyhouse recently, one place in front of today's rival Jazzy Dancer; almost two years since his last win; might struggle to record another success in the closing stages of his career.
runners.comment: Fair sixth at Fairyhouse on his 100th start, almost two years since his last win
runners.owner: Paul Cullen & Joseph E Keeling & M C Grassick

runners.name: Half Nutz
runners.spotlight: Six wins to his name but on a losing run that stretches back to September 2022; not beaten far in two of her last four races (all at Gowran) but clearly needs to find extra; official rating has slumped from a peak of 85 to a lowly 38.
runners.comment: Six wins to his name but on a losing run that stretches back to September 2022

runners.name: Linger For Longer
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner; below par last year; faded in a 1m contest at this event on her first run of the season; more like it when fourth at Listowel ten days ago; now reunited with the champion jockey who won twice on her in 2024; worth considering.
runners.comment: Three-time winner, below par last year, more like it when fourth at Listowel ten days ago

runners.name: Dinamine
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m at Bellewstown last August, adding to an early-season success over 7f at Gowran; lost ground at the start when reappearing at Navan, compromising his chance over a 6f trip that was probably inadequate in any case.
runners.comment: Won over 1m at Bellewstown last August, lost ground at the start on 6f seasonal debut

runners.name: Zabriskie Point
runners.spotlight: Two wins came as a juvenile for Charlie Hills, at 5f/7f on different ground types; has plunged in the ratings. took third behind Exceeding at Leopardstown in April; unplaced favourite at Gowran next time; runner-up to an in-form rival at Roscommon before fifth at Listowel; capable of getting in the mix.
runners.comment: Has been showing a consistent level of form this season, capable of getting in the mix
runners.owner: B White & M W White & Ms H McGuinness & D Sheridan

runners.name: Methgal
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough in a 7f Dundalk handicap in March and followed that with runner-up finishes over the same trip back at Dundalk and on turf at Leopardstown (beaten by Exceeding); below his best at the Curragh early last month and in a recent Listowel race contested by several of these rivals.
runners.comment: Below best in races at the Curragh and Listowel since second to Exceeding at Leopardstown

runners.name: Exceeding
runners.spotlight: No luck in running in the Listowel event in which Linger For Longer and Zabriskie Point finished in front of him; had previously shown improved form to beat Methgal narrowly at Leopardstown, with Zabriskie Point close up in the third; clear chance on that form.
runners.comment: No luck in running at Listowel, better judged on Leopardstown win form two of these rivals

runners.name: Unfamiliar
runners.spotlight: Has failed to make the frame in seven attempts; best effort so far at Fairyhouse last Friday, racing prominently until weakening late into fifth in a 17-runner 6f contest; interesting that she is turned again so soon.
runners.comment: Faded late into fifth in a 17-runner 6f contest last Friday, quick return is  interesting

runners.name: Concaire
runners.spotlight: Signs of improvement on handicap debut when sixth of 14 at Roscommon a month ago; further progress is feasible.
runners.comment: Signs of improvement when sixth of 14 at Roscommon, further progress is feasible

runners.name: Tynamite
runners.spotlight: Veteran is a six-time AW winner but 0-34 on turf; no success since December 2023; down the field in two starts this season.
runners.comment: Veteran, six-time AW winner, 0-34 on turf, no success since December 2023, poor this term

runners.name: Harry Longabaugh
runners.spotlight: Modest form in maidens; beat only one rival over 5f on handicap/seasonal debut at Naas; not much better in a 6f Fairyhouse contest.
runners.comment: Beat only one rival  on handicap/seasonal debut at Naas, not much better at Fairyhouse

runners.name: Gobi Star
runners.spotlight: Better record on AW when at his peak; very little merit in either AW or turf form in seven outings since last December, when he returned after a lengthy absence.
runners.comment: Very little merit in either AW or turf form in seven outings since last December
runners.owner: Patrick J Digan & Ms Yvonne Hennessy

runners.name: Pick A Window
runners.spotlight: No sign of worthwhile form in five maiden outings; makes handicap debut off a lowly mark; step up in trip may be some help.
runners.comment: Weak form, makes handicap debut off a lowly mark, step up in trip may be some help

runners.name: Sosperi
runners.spotlight: Hard to take any encouragement from last season's form, blinkered for the first time; trainer had a good day at Navan on Sunday.
runners.comment: Hard to take any encouragement from last season's form, blinkered for the first time

runners.name: Run Forrest Run
runners.spotlight: 6-45 on turf but losing run stretches back to October 2024; poor form on AW last winter; shaped reasonably well in mid-field at Listowel ten days on first run for Laura Hourigan.
runners.comment: 6-45 on turf, last success was in October 2024, not a bad run at Listowel on stable debut

runners.name: Green Icon
runners.spotlight: Both wins came over 7f for Owen Burrows early in his career; yet to find his form for this yard; gave a mildly encouraging display in finishing in midfield when a 66-1 chance at the Curragh last week.
runners.comment: Mildly encouraging mid-field finish when a 66-1 chance at the Curragh last week.

runners.name: Autumn Twist
runners.spotlight: Ballinrobe maiden (1m2f) win last summer sandwiched by two hurdle sucesses, all on good ground; form this spring below-best under both codes however and unproven over this far.
runners.comment: Below-best under both codes of late and unproven over this far

runners.name: Tatum
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed an excellent campaign last season which included two wins at Leopardstown (1m4f/1m5f, good); 1m2f too sharp on return there last month, interesting now upped further in trip here with Keane aboard.
runners.comment: All the better for recent comeback run over inadequate trip; Keane booked

runners.name: Loyal Touch
runners.spotlight: Five-time Flat winner in Britain at up to 1m2f; not at same level for this yard, well beaten over hurdles last twice.
runners.comment: Yet to hit form for this yard, trip an unknown too

runners.name: Quiz Test
runners.spotlight: Sole win came at Cork in 2023; decent form last autumn, including a close third over 1m4f at Galway and second in a 19-runner contest at the Curragh; well-backed on latest of two runs this term when fourth at Gowran (Good Onya Mate second); 5lb claimer now rides but more exposed than that rival.
runners.comment: Over 2l to find with Good Onya Mate from Gowran and the more exposed of the pair

runners.name: Good Onya Mate
runners.spotlight: Nicely improved on second handicap attempt when enterprisingly-ridden over 1m6f at Gowran last month, green but rallying after being headed to finish a clear second (Quiz Test fourth); good chance he'll get this trip and likely more to offer.
runners.comment: Gowran second could have more to offer at this longer trip; big chance
runners.owner: P J Morgan & Mrs S L Morgan

runners.name: Riyami
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner at 2m; 0-12 on turf and recent form under both codes leaves him with a bit to find.
runners.comment: Four-time AW winner at 2m; recent form under both codes leaves him with a bit to find

runners.name: Miss Australie
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 15th attempt in 1m4f Roscommon handicap last month (yielding-to-soft), coming from off the pace; 5lb rise so more needed but respected.
runners.comment: Recent Roscommon winner; 5lb rise so more needed but respected

runners.name: Nod Of Approval
runners.spotlight: Best run yet on latest when upped to 1m4f in Cork handicap in April, coming from good bit back after tardy start when second to an improver (won again next time); half-brother to Irish Cesarewitch winner Puturhandstogether could well improve again now upped further in distance.
runners.comment: Cork second reads well and longer trip could bring him into his own

runners.name: Bynx
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner (also chase winner) is 0-21 on the Flat but went close over this trip at Thurles last autumn off today's mark; can't rule out.
runners.comment: Better NH performer but has run well off this mark before; can't rule out

runners.name: Pass The Buck
runners.spotlight: Recent Ballinrobe maiden run was okay but will have to improve a fair bit for this step up in trip to make an impact on second handicap attempt (3lb wrong).
runners.comment: Improvement needed for this step up in trip from 3lb out of the handicap

runners.name: Mr Rango
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner at 1m2f on soft or heavy, latest over two years ago; fair form this season but good bit to find with a couple of these on recent Leopardstown run and remains unproven over this extended trip.
runners.comment: Bit to find with a couple of these on Leopardstown run; remains unproven over this trip

runners.name: Ellaat
runners.spotlight: Dual purpose 10yo whose Flat wins include 1m4f handicap here; comes here in good form after hurdle second at Ballinrobe last month off a fair mark.
runners.comment: Winner here over 1m4f; good recent hurdles run puts him in the mix

runners.name: Our Lucky Lady
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler returned to Flat recently to justify strong market support when hooded first-time in 2m Down Royal handicap under today's rider, beating Sunday's Navan winner Jurality with some authority; hit with 12lb rise but could well play a role.
runners.comment: Down Royal win franked by second winning at Navan on Sunday; 12lb higher but respected

runners.name: Almenfee
runners.spotlight: German winner has failed to fire so far for this yard; declining mark but still best watched.
runners.comment: German winner has failed to fire so far for this yard; declining mark but best watched

runners.name: Karthon
runners.spotlight: Formerly with Andrew Balding; has been placed at up to 12.5f in 5-10 starts; didn't appear to have any obvious excuses when beaten 7l when upped to this trip here latest; that's clearly not put connections off trying again and he's well treated on some of last year's form.
runners.comment: Placed in 5-1o starts; beaten 7l over C&D latest but is well treated on some 2025 form.

runners.name: Fleurman
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner, mostly at about 2m and is 1lb higher than when scoring over C&D in March; matched that effort back here next time, before a below-par effort on soft at Haydock latest; will be a player if bouncing back.
runners.comment: C&D winner; below par on soft latest but will be a player if bouncing back.

runners.name: Saratoga Gold
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner at up to 1m6f, including here but hasn't won for almost three years; even so, has been placed on five successive occasions and is capable of going close from this mark now back on this surface.
runners.comment: Placed on five successive occasions and capable of going close from this mark back on AW.

runners.name: Vilaine Verlaine
runners.spotlight: 1m2f Polytrack winner; takes a big step up in trip for this and will certainly have stamina to prove, even if shrugging off an inauspicious start to the campaign.
runners.comment: Has stamina to prove, even if shrugging off an inauspicious start to the campaign.

runners.name: Further Measure
runners.spotlight: Prolific winner, mostly on AW and at 1m6f-2m, including C&D; 1lb higher than when scoring here in February; not quite as good in two subsequent starts but looks a player back up in trip and with the same claimer up as for that most recent success.
runners.comment: 1lb higher than February's C&D win and looks a player with same claimer booked for this.

runners.name: Tarbat Ness
runners.spotlight: Thorough stayer whose wins have all been at about 2m, his two AW successes coming on Tapeta; 4lb higher than the most recent of those and has each-way claims.
runners.comment: Thorough stayer; 4lb higher than his most recent success and has each-way claims.

runners.name: Rogue Empire
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at the eighth attempt with an authoritative performance in a 0-60 at Lingfield (2m, good) last month; 6lb rise looks fair and he has run well over this C&D; unexposed and warrants respect with 3lb being claimed.
runners.comment: Up 6lb for authoritative win at Lingfield; unexposed; respected with 3lb claimer up.

runners.name: Individualiste
runners.spotlight: Formerly with Paul Nicholls then Gavin Cromwell; has four jumps wins to his name; placed twice in this code at up to 1m5f but very hard to fancy on his form since joining his current trainer.
runners.comment: Placed twice but very hard to fancy on his form since joining his current trainer.

runners.name: Pequenita
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam French 1m-1m3f winner (including Group 2; RPR 111), sister to 1m6f Group 3 winner Master Of Reality, closely related to 1m4f Listed winner Chamonix; trainer 11% with AW 2yos (last five seasons); should be capable of a good showing on debut.
runners.comment: Dam French 1m-1m3f winner (including Group 2; RPR 111); one of the likelier types.

runners.name: Virtue Charity
runners.spotlight: 40-1 for debut at Goodwood (6f, good) last month, when always behind and beaten 10l; much more needed.
runners.comment: 40-1 for debut at Goodwood, when always behind and beaten 10l; much more needed.

runners.name: Dark Reign
runners.spotlight: First foal; unraced dam closely related to 1m1f Group 3 winner King Of Cities, half-sister to 6f Group 1 winner Inisherin, out of 1m2f Group 1 winning close relative of 1m Group 1 winner Triple Time; trainer 9% with AW 2yos (last five seasons); check the market for clues.
runners.comment: First foal; unraced dam closely related to 1m1f Group 3 winner King Of Cities.
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Agent Rose
runners.spotlight: 28,000euros yearling, 90,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; seventh foal; half-sister to five winners including Mystical Dawn (1m 2yo; RPR 97), Tamam Desert (7f 2yo; 86) and Cosmic Soul (2m Flat including AW, 78; 2m1f/2m4f hurdle); dam 1m-1m2f winner (102); trainer 20% with AW 2yos (last five seasons); market check suggested.
runners.comment: Half-sister to five winners; dam 1m-1m2f winner; trainer 20% with AW 2yos last five years.
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xxxi & J Morley

runners.name: Lady Caroline Lamb
runners.spotlight: Second foal; sister to 1m 2yo winner Magic Trip (RPR 83); dam 1m4f/1m6f AW winner (78), half-sister to useful winners Galapiat (1m4f) and Madame Ambassador (1m2f Listed); trainer 17% with AW 2yos (last five seasons); market check suggested.
runners.comment: Sister to 1m 2yo winner Magic Trip (RPR 83); trainer 17% with AW 2yos last five years.

runners.name: Sunset Script
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam unraced sister to Listed-placed 1m3f winner Rouge Sellier, closely related to winners Lumiere (Cheveley Park) and Sheikha Reika (Canadian 1m2f Grade 1); check the betting.
runners.comment: Dam unraced sister to Listed-placed 1m3f winner Rouge Sellier; check the betting.

runners.name: Guadalevin
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to five winners, out of a 1m2f winner; well supported on debut when 9-4 and a close third at Southwell (7f, Tapeta); that's modest form but she should be better for the experience and could take a step up on it.
runners.comment: Well backed on debut when a close third; modest form but could take a step up on it.

runners.name: Woodhay Wayfarer
runners.spotlight: 28-1 for debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm) last month, when never better than midfield; needs a big step up on that.
runners.comment: 28-1 for debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm), when never better than midfield.

runners.name: Rosevannion
runners.spotlight: 14-1 for debut, when always prominent or leading until the final furlong and 4l fourth at Wolverhampton (7f, Tapeta); should be capable of building on that.
runners.comment: Prominent until the closing stages on debut (7f, Tapeta); can build on that.
runners.owner: C Campbell, Ballylinch Stud & Brook Stud

runners.name: Velvet Vega
runners.spotlight: Weak 9-2 for debut at Newmarket (1m2f, good) last month, when she showed signs of greenness but came 5l second of seven; that form looks very good in this context and she could prove tough to beat with any improvement.
runners.comment: Green when second at Newmarket (1m2f, good); that looks good form; the one to beat.

runners.name: Tribal Queen
runners.spotlight: Frankel half-sister to a 1m2f/1m4f winner (including Listed); 100-30 for debut at Haydock (1m2f, soft) last month, when slow to get going but getting the hang of things as the race went on and beaten under 3l; with improvement for that experience, she'll be a player.
runners.comment: Frankel half-sister to a middle-distance winner; beaten under 3l on debut; likely player.

runners.name: Salve Allegra
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam 7f-1m6f maiden, out of maiden half-sister to US 8.5f Grade 2 winner Salve Germania, is granddaughter of German Oaks winner; trainer not known for his AW 2yo winners; hooded on debut; best watched.
runners.comment: Dam 7f-1m6f maiden; trainer not known for AW 2yo winners; hooded on debut; best watched.

runners.name: Gypsy Jazz Queen
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister to 6f/7f AW winner Main Target (including 2yo; RPR 78); dam 1m-1m2f winner (including AW/Group 2; 113), half-sister to 1m2f Group 1 winner Izzi Top; dam won here; trainer 18% with AW 2yos; interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: Half-sister to 6f/7f AW winner out of 1m-1m2f winner; trainer 18% with AW 2yos.

runners.name: Sullom Voe
runners.spotlight: Looks the pick of the three saddled by this trainer and has shown some ability despite being ultimately well beaten at both Sandown and Salisbury (both 1m2f, good); likely to be of greater interest when entering handicaps.
runners.comment: Has shown some ability despite being well beaten; should appeal more in handicaps.

runners.name: Palomezcal
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at big odds in both starts; much more needed.
runners.comment: Well beaten at big odds in both starts; much more needed.

runners.name: Persian Flaw
runners.spotlight: 50-1 for debut at Salisbury (1m2f, good) last month when slowly away and never seriously involved; more needed.
runners.comment: 50-1 for debut at Salisbury (1m2f, good) last month when slowly away and never involved.

runners.name: Midnight Steppers
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at increasing odds in all three starts; watching brief advised.
runners.comment: Well beaten at increasing odds in all three starts; watching brief advised.

runners.name: Criminal
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden; had a near miss dropped into Class 5 company at Lingfield in April (1m2f); fair run on turf last month despite finishing last of the four runners; each-way shout.
runners.comment: 12-race maiden; creditable efforts over 1m2f on last two starts; each-way claims

runners.name: Lady Manzor
runners.spotlight: Handicapped to win and she ran creditably over C&D three weeks ago following a tardy start; cheekpieces left off today; not discounted.
runners.comment: Handicapped to win and latest C&D 4th was encouraging; headgear absent today; chance

runners.name: I Am Me
runners.spotlight: Off the mark for this yard with a last-gasp C&D win in February; a 3lb rise for that nose success leaves him vulnerable.
runners.comment: Last-gasp C&D win in February; a 3lb rise for that narrow success leaves him vulnerable

runners.name: American State
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins this year; met plenty of trouble when down the field at Newcastle last week but still makes only limited appeal tonight.
runners.comment: Two AW wins this year; not at best on last two runs, albeit met trouble latest

runners.name: Bold Suitor
runners.spotlight: C&D win last summer; best effort for some time when getting up late to beat Havana Touch by a nose at Lingfield six weeks ago (7f, AW); a 2lb rise shouldn't prevent another prominent showing.
runners.comment: C&D winner; led late when beating Havana Touch at Lingfield (7f) in April; contender again

runners.name: Billy Mill
runners.spotlight: Prolific winner who arrives on the back of three placed efforts over C&D; now 2lb higher but likely to give his running once again.
runners.comment: Placed in C&D handicaps on his last three starts; likely to go well despite revised mark

runners.name: Havana Touch
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism in a C&D handicap in April off 5lb lower; beaten a nose at Lingfield later that month and then met trouble in running at Ripon last week; should remain competitive.
runners.comment: C&D winner in April; two solid efforts have followed; should remain competitive

runners.name: Hour By Hour
runners.spotlight: Well treated on his 2024 form; low-key stable debut here three weeks ago and he arrives with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Well treated on 2024 form but he needs to leave his low-key stable debut well behind

runners.name: My Boy Harry
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; arrives in form, winning easily over 7f at Lingfield (good ground); solid third at Brighton 12 days ago and returning to 1m is no issue; this is a step up in class though.
runners.comment: C&D winner; arrives in good form but he needs a bit more to come out on top upped in class

runners.name: Just Typical
runners.spotlight: 1m turf win last September off 4lb lower; in and out since, often conceding ground at the start, but Ryan Kavanagh takes off a valuable 5lb; below his best in one Kempton appearance.
runners.comment: Chance if returning at his best but he failed to fire in one Kempton appearance

runners.name: Island Hero
runners.spotlight: Promising second in a Wolverhampton novice (8.6f) last January but he's struggled in both subsequent runs, including on handicap debut at Sandown (1m, soft) last August; still unexposed but he has plenty to prove after another absence; hooded first time.
runners.comment: Absent since finishing down the field on handicap debut 285 days ago; returns wearing hood

runners.name: Marengo Storm
runners.spotlight: Stepped up on his debut (C&D) when readily accounting for nine rivals in a C&D maiden last month; couldn't reward favourite backers at Windsor on his handicap debut 18 days ago but it was still a sound effort in second, keeping on well in the final furlong (third has won since); up 3lb but doubt he has reached his ceiling.
runners.comment: C&D winner last month; good second on turf/handicap debut latest; up 3lb but more to come

runners.name: Jumeirah Sands
runners.spotlight: Market springer on her 2yo debut but on the back foot after a slow start and could only manage fifth; overcame another slow start to win a Southwell novice in April (6f) and she was sharper away when overcoming a penalty back at Southwell five weeks ago; needs more now handicapping and she's drawn widest but her ceiling may not have been reached.
runners.comment: Won two 6f novices at Southwell this spring; this is tougher but could have more to give

runners.name: The Lost Sock
runners.spotlight: Won a 5f maiden at Catterick last summer; expensive to follow in handicaps this year and the step back up to 6f isn't enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Expensive to follow in handicaps this year; returning to 6f not enough to tempt

runners.name: Angel Love
runners.spotlight: Couldn't go overboard about the form of her Wolverhampton maiden win (6f) last November, for all it was by a wide margin; drawn wide when down the field at Chester on her seasonal/handicap debut five weeks ago; better berthed today but perhaps too high in the weights.
runners.comment: AW maiden win doesn't look strong form; better drawn today than at Chester last month

runners.name: Deluded
runners.spotlight: 7f AW win last September; good second at Wolverhampton on his reappearance but he was a well-beaten third at Newmarket last month (7f, good); others look stronger.
runners.comment: Drop back to 6f may help but others look stronger for win purposes

runners.name: Arry Up
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2yo campaign with a Leicester nursery win; further progress with his two C&D wins in early April; well beaten in a big-field Ascot handicap in his bid for a four-timer last month; career-best effort required.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins to start 2026 but well beaten at Ascot latest; career best needed

runners.name: Moonshine
runners.spotlight: Promise in her first three starts; off the mark at the fourth attempt when winning a small-field fillies' novice over C&D in March; no improvement for handicaps at Yarmouth (7f, good to firm) six weeks ago but still ran to form; others perhaps better treated.
runners.comment: C&D winner; fair third on handicap debut (7f, good to firm) six weeks ago; needs more

runners.name: Siren Suit
runners.spotlight: Finished a close third in a hot race at Ascot last April (closely matched with Angel Of Anfield) on his second start and had little trouble in seeing off eight rivals in a 7f maiden here last October; his two runs since, seven months apart, have resulted in heavy defeats; drops in class and could make a bold bid back on AW with Toby Moore taking off a handy 7lb.
runners.comment: Of interest on 2yo best, including a course win (7f); drops in class; good claimer booked

runners.name: Rogue Supremacy
runners.spotlight: Two wins for David O'Meara as a 2yo and also ran a cracker over C&D last September (Group 3); the handicapper has been in command this year but he returns to AW and drops back to a Class 4; not solid but a revival isn't out of the question.
runners.comment: Best run came over C&D; handicapper on top this year but now drops in class

runners.name: Big Harry
runners.spotlight: Improved with each of his three 2yo runs, making all for a comfortable success at Doncaster in November (6f, heavy); that form has been advertised by the second and third and he moves into handicaps off a tempting mark; absent seven months but well drawn and he's an interesting contender.
runners.comment: 2yo win at Doncaster franked since; opening mark tempting but absent for seven months

runners.name: Generous Rascal
runners.spotlight: C&D win for Oliver Cole last October; he hasn't run badly two 6f handicaps for this yard but improvement is needed now returned to AW.
runners.comment: C&D winner for forrmer yard; retains ability but a career best is needed to win

runners.name: Sayidah Hard Spun
runners.spotlight: She ran well in both C&D starts last year, winning a fillies' novice last May and a close fifth in a Group 3 in September; well held in two turf handicaps this year, the latest nine days ago, but drops in class today and a revival isn't out of the question.
runners.comment: Two solid efforts over C&D last year; yet to shine in 2026 but drops in class today

runners.name: Exotic Baby
runners.spotlight: 1m win as a 2yo; the handicapper has held the upper hand since and hopes appear to rest on the drop to 6f having a positive effect.
runners.comment: Consistent on AW but hopes appear to rest on the drop to 6f having a positive effect

runners.name: Seu Jo
runners.spotlight: 1m AW win in France in March; big prices and well beaten in two runs for new connections; may need some more help from the handicapper.
runners.comment: Big prices and well beaten in two runs for new connections; may need some more weight off

runners.name: Amazing Journey
runners.spotlight: Two 6f wins last summer but things haven't gone to plan since, often compromising his chance at the start; last month's return to action at Leicester was promising, though, and he's almost certainly better than this mark should the stars align.
runners.comment: Doesn't help himself at the start but talented, and his reappearance was promising; chance

runners.name: Front Line Fury
runners.spotlight: Not matched his 2yo best in two runs this year but he drops into a Class 4 and today's switch to AW could suit; well treated if reviving.
runners.comment: Not matched his 2yo form so far this year but he now drops into a Class 4 for his AW debut

runners.name: Sir Alfie
runners.spotlight: Won twice for Ger Lyons last summer, making all in a 6f handicap at the Curragh last August; well beaten in two runs for Charles Byrnes last autumn and he hasn't fired in three runs for his new connections either; latest York run was slightly more encouraging so he's worth a market check dropping into a Class 4 and returning to 6f.
runners.comment: Struggled since his Irish win last August; down in class; market should guide

runners.name: Club Class
runners.spotlight: Gained a breakthrough victory over C&D in September and she has resumed in good form, fourth of seven over C&D last month; this Frankel filly may do better still (lost shoe last time) so is a likely player eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Gained breakthrough C&D win in September and she has resumed in good form; likely player

runners.name: Caramay
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways over 1m3f here in February and in good form since, not seen to ideal effect when fifth of nine in 1m4f Goodwood handicap 19 days ago; she ought to be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Resumed winning ways here in February and in good form since; ought to be thereabouts

runners.name: Desert Belle
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at the second attempt in 1m4f Southwell novice in April; still looked a work in progress when fourth of five on her handicap debut at Wolverhampton (1m4f) 23 days ago; this Masar filly remains with potential.
runners.comment: Still looked work in progress with Wolverhampton fourth latest; remains with potential

runners.name: Golden Muse
runners.spotlight: Bagged back-to-back novices at Wolverhampton (9.5f) and Southwell (1m4f) in March before advancing her form on her first go in handicaps with a clear third of six at Doncaster (1m2f) in April; this Golden Horn filly has more to offer, especially now back up in trip; major player.
runners.comment: Scored at Wolverhampton and Southwell; excellent third at Doncaster latest; major player

runners.name: Too Farhh Gone
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive daughter of Farhh; gained a first win in 1m3f Southwell maiden in April and backed it up with a good fourth of ten on her handicap debut at Salisbury (1m4f) 18 days ago; not out of things on her Polytrack debut.
runners.comment: Won at Southwell; good fourth on h'cap debut at Salisbury since; not out of things

runners.name: Shushi
runners.spotlight: Camelot filly who has made the frame on all her four runs, posting a fair fourth on her handicap debut at Doncaster (1m4f) last time; no forlorn hope off a 3lb lower mark.
runners.comment: Fair fourth on h'cap debut at Doncaster last time; no forlorn hope off a 3lb lower mark

runners.name: Possible
runners.spotlight: Fair ex-French maiden (stays 2m) for Francis-Henri Graffard in the first half of 2025; off ten months before coming in a well-held ninth for her new yard in 1m4f Newbury handicap 26 days ago; should strip sharper here.
runners.comment: Ex-French maiden; off ten months before a well-held ninth at Newbury; should strip sharper

runners.name: Soleil d'Arizona
runners.spotlight: Ex-French 1m3f Flat scorer; a fair 2m winning hurdler for her current yard in 2025 but she was tailed off in 2m Ludlow handicap hurdle last month; needs to take a big step forward on her first Flat run since 2023.
runners.comment: Ex-French 1m3f Flat scorer; tailed off over hurdles last month so needs a big step forward

runners.name: Jindri
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who got off the mark at the sixth attempt in 1m4f handicap at Southwell in November; off since so she needs to hit the ground running on her seasonal return.
runners.comment: Off mark at Southwell in November; off since so she needs to hit the ground running

runners.name: Golspie
runners.spotlight: Kept busy in 2026 and running well without winning, in the frame for the sixth time with third of nine in 1m2f Haydock handicap 19 days ago; not out of things on his Polytrack debut.
runners.comment: In the frame for sixth time in 2026 with Haydock third 19 days ago; not out of things

runners.name: Karakula
runners.spotlight: Gained a first win at Wolverhampton (9.5f) in September; has resumed in excellent form, not enjoying the smoothest of passages when beaten a short head in another 9.5f Wolverhampton handicap 23 days ago; a likely player back up in trip.
runners.comment: Not smoothest of runs when beaten short head at Wolverhampton latest; a likely player

runners.name: Sonnerie Power
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start for current yard over C&D in January and has continued in good nick, set a lot to do when sixth of 15 in 1m4f Lingfield handicap in April; in the mix once more.
runners.comment: Winning start for current yard over C&D and has continued in good nick; in the mix again

runners.name: Sea Of Charm
runners.spotlight: Kickstarted 2026 with 1m4f AW victory at Lingfield but her more recent form is not so encouraging, sixth of eight in 1m4f Chepstow handicap 12 days ago; more is required in a change of headgear.
runners.comment: Kickstarted 2026 with 1m4f AW Lingfield win but her more recent form is not so encouraging

runners.name: Pershalla
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over 1m4f here in April but she came in a below-par seventh of ten in 11.5f Windsor handicap 30 days ago; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Off the mark here in April but below-par seventh at Windsor latest; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Uzincso
runners.spotlight: Veteran who garnered a seventh victory here over C&D in September; took a step back in the right direction with fourth of 11 in 1m4f handicap here three weeks ago; well in the mix eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Resurgent fourth here three weeks ago; this course specialist is well in the mix here

runners.name: Imperial Cult
runners.spotlight: Gained a first win since 2024 when landing gamble for his new yard in 1m4f handicap at Lingfield in April; back up 3lb but remains handily weighted on past form so merits serious consideration.
runners.comment: Landed gamble for new yard at Lingfield in April; up 3lb but merits serious consideration

runners.name: Wood Whisperer
runners.spotlight: Showed some fair form for Paul Attwater in 2025 but has failed to build on it for his present yard, going very freely when 12th in 1m2f handicap at Nottingham in April; has since been gelded.
runners.comment: Fair form for Paul Attwater in 2025; has failed to build on it for present yard; gelded

runners.name: Semele
runners.spotlight: Modest form shown in three bumpers; has twice failed to build on an encouraging C&D maiden fourth in March, coming in last of ten in 1m2f Nottingham novice 22 days ago; lots more required now going handicapping.
runners.comment: Twice failed to build on encouraging C&D fourth in March; lots more needed on h'cap debut
runners.owner: Mr And Mrs A E Pakenham

runners.name: Running The Game
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in January, 2024; lightly raced since though and has his fitness to prove returning from 15 months off; the market can guide.
runners.comment: C&D winner in 2024; lightly raced since and has his fitness to prove after 15 months off

runners.name: St Just In Time
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden and he beat only one on his return from 15 months off for his new yard in 1m3f Southwell handicap 20 days ago; needs to take a major step forward.
runners.comment: Beat only one on return from 15 months off for new yard in Southwell handicap 20 days ago

runners.name: Three Yorkshiremen
runners.spotlight: Took this in 2025 for Michael Wigham; also won for Barry Brennan at Wolverhampton in November but well below par in three runs for that yard this season; starts out for another stable here with something to prove.
runners.comment: Took this in 2025 for Michael Wigham; well below par this season for Barry Brennan though

runners.name: Blackwaterfoot
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark in 1m2f Ffos Las handicap but well below that form in handicaps (around 1m2f) at Wolverhampton and Nottingham for Paul Attwater later last summer; needs yard switch to spark a resurgence after seven months off.
runners.comment: Won at Ffos Las but ended time with Paul Attwater below par; more needed from new yard

runners.name: Man From Havana
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced Havana Grey gelding; went too freely when seventh of 11 in 1m Nottingham maiden in October; returns for his handicap debut with more needed.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced Havana Grey gelding; returns for his handicap debut with more needed
runners.owner: P J & Mrs J P Haycock

runners.name: Summerstorms Dream
runners.spotlight: Slow starts have been a recurring theme since she moved into handicaps but she has hinted that her mark is in range; cheekpieces added and Eireann Cagney is booked, so there can be encouragement for an improved showing tonight.
runners.comment: Clear promise handicapping; slow starts not ideal but good amateur booked and headgear on

runners.name: No Nay Nevermind
runners.spotlight: 0-19; runner-up twice in 7f AW handicaps in late 2025 but this year's efforts leave her with a point to prove.
runners.comment: 0-19 and she has been well held on her last three starts; others look stronger
runners.owner: S Jones, L Donaldson & K Elliott

runners.name: Penny Mountain
runners.spotlight: 6f AW win on New Year's Day; several good runs in defeat have followed and dropping back to 6f could prove beneficial.
runners.comment: 6f AW win in January; creditable efforts in defeat since; now reverts to sprinting

runners.name: Mister Sky Blue
runners.spotlight: C&D win last May (good to firm) remains his only win from 20 starts; he has run two solid races in defeat this year, however, and would seem highly likely to run his race once more.
runners.comment: C&D winner last May; two solid efforts this year; likely to give his running again

runners.name: Doon The Glen
runners.spotlight: Just one win from 33 starts but he has twice finished second this year (both 6f, AW and good to firm); return to sprinting can help, as should the return of a visor, and he helps form a strong hand for Jim Goldie.
runners.comment: Infrequent winner but tonight's return to 6f looks in his favour; visor returns too

runners.name: Pebble Dash
runners.spotlight: 0-15 but the odd promising effort, including a close second at Newcastle in March (7f); neither of his turf runs this summer suggest he is the answer today.
runners.comment: 0-15; 2nd over 7f (AW) in March but his recent turf efforts need bettering

runners.name: Shoptilyoudrop
runners.spotlight: Exposed 21-race maiden; ran one of her better races when fourth at Wolverhampton in February when last seen but still easy enough to swerve.
runners.comment: Exposed 21-race maiden; ran okay when last seen but still looks vulnerable
runners.owner: James And Susan Cookson And P Kirby

runners.name: Royal Heritage
runners.spotlight: 98,000gns yearling, 800,000euros breeze-up 2yo; seventh foal from unraced half-sister to US 1m Grade 3 winner Farhaan; closely related to 7f-1m winner Lattam (RPR 102) and a half-brother to three winners; connections had a promising juvenile winner (Alta Regina) at Lingfield recently.
runners.comment: 800,000euros breeze-up 2yo; dam unraced half-sister to US 1m Grade3 winner Farhaan

runners.name: My Mate Fitz
runners.spotlight: March foal; £75,000 vendor yearling; first foal from US Graded stakes-placed 5.5f turf 2yo winner Invincible Gal (RPR 98); already gelded; trainer has made a flying start this term with his juveniles running at over a 30% strike-rate, National Stakes winner Where Love Lives the most notable.
runners.comment: March foal; first foal from US Graded stakes-placed 5.5f turf 2yo winner Invincible Gal.

runners.name: Casino Star
runners.spotlight: March foal; £100,000 yearling; third foal from a Listed-placed 5f 2yo and 7f winner; half-brother to the 5f 2yo winner Bacor (RPR 75); top trainer is running at a 27% strike-rate with juveniles this year (20% over the past five years); high on the shortlist.
runners.comment: March foal; £100,000 yearling; third foal of Listed-placed 5f 2yo and 7f winner; shortlist

runners.name: Desert Move
runners.spotlight: March foal; third foal from a 6f juvenile winner (RPR 92); half-brother to the 1m2f AW winner Sharp Move; joint trainers have had three juvenile winners (11% strike-rate) this year, including Crystal Queen (same ownership) in the Hilary Needler at Beverley; market informative.
runners.comment: March foal; third foal from 6f juvenile winner; half-brother to 1m2f AW winner Sharp Move.

runners.name: Sea Palace
runners.spotlight: February foal; 92,000gns foal, 120,000gns yearling; ninth foal from a 5f 2yo winner (including Listed; RPR 105); half-brother to five winners, including Nigel Nott (5f-6f AW; 95); trainer seeking first juvenile winner (0-9) of the season.
runners.comment: 92,000gns foal, 120,000gns yearling; dam 5f 2yo winner (including Listed; RPR 105)

runners.name: Glory Of The Seas
runners.spotlight: 550,000gns yearling; steady improvement; beaten just over 7l (fourth) by the exciting Water To Wine on seasonal reappearance at Newbury (1m3f, good), before finishing third in a hot maiden at Chester's May Festival (12.5f maiden); gelded since and this looks the perfect opportunity to strike.
runners.comment: Third in a hot maiden at Chester in May (12.5f, maiden); gelded since; the one to beat.

runners.name: Yafreh
runners.spotlight: Brother to 7f-1m2f winner Al Mubhir (RPR 115); showed similar level of ability in his two starts, last time 6-1 when a rallying fourth at Haydock (11.5f, soft; Florida Dreams fifth) three weeks ago; slightly more required and he will soon be handicapping.
runners.comment: Fourth at Haydock (11.5f; soft) three weeks ago; slightly more required.

runners.name: Aussie Pearl
runners.spotlight: In the mix on Leicester debut (1m maiden fillies; 7-2) last October but soundly beaten by a long odds-on favourite at Newmarket (1m2f, good) a fortnight later; off since and, even receiving the fillies' allowance, this looks a tough race in which to reappear; stamina to prove.
runners.comment: Third to a long odds-on favourite at Newmarket (1m2f) last October; stamina to prove.

runners.name: Florida Dreams
runners.spotlight: Grade 2 bumper winner in April 2023, who also won twice over hurdles (2m) for Nicky Richards; 22-1, lacked the pace to challenge when fifth of nine on Flat debut at Haydock (11.5f, soft; Yafreh about 1l ahead in fourth) three weeks ago; likely to be seen in a better light once handicapping.
runners.comment: Lacked the pace to challenge on Flat debut at Haydock (11.5f; fifth, 22-1) three weeks ago

runners.name: Togeather Forever
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to four winners, including the Listed scorer Ralston Road (1m2f-1m6f); slowly into stride and always behind on debut here (1m1f novice; hooded, 40-1) a fortnight ago; this longer trip should suit but time will be his ultimate friend; highly unlikely candidate.
runners.comment: Slowly into stride and always behind on debut here (1m1f novice; 40-1) a fortnight ago.

runners.name: Canaria Queen
runners.spotlight: In-form mare; twice successful this season, including at Musselburgh (5f, Class 6, good; from 5lb lower); runner-up at Ripon on last two outings (Tees Aggregates a close third on penultimate start); has no issues with this track; merits respect.
runners.comment: Won at Musselburgh (5f; Class 6) last month; runner-up at Ripon twice since; in-form mare.

runners.name: Hi Lord
runners.spotlight: Headgear removed when he pounced late to beat Mount Ruapehu over C&D (good; from 3lb lower) on penultimate start; ran to an identical level on RPRs when third here last week; should give his running at a track (form figures 2613) which suits.
runners.comment: Pounced late to claim Mount Ruapehu over C&D (good; from 3lb lower) on penultimate start.

runners.name: Mount Ruapehu
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism at Southwell (6f) in April and pipped by Hi Lord over C&D (good) the following week; less competitive at Catterick (7f) and Carlisle (6f) on last two outings and the handicapper might have his measure.
runners.comment: Pipped by Hi Lord over C&D in May; far less competitive elsewhere (6f-7f) since.

runners.name: Tees Aggregates
runners.spotlight: Didn't have things go smoothly but stuck on positively down the outer when closing third (Canaria Queen second) at Ripon (5f, good) a fortnight ago; two of his three wins have been at tracks with a stiff uphill finish (Beverley and Leicester) and he's handicapped to go close at the least.
runners.comment: Stuck on positively when third (Canaria Queen second) at Ripon a fortnight ago; player.
runners.owner: Ingleby Bloodstock Ltd & The Ivy League

runners.name: Pal Joey
runners.spotlight: Won at Newcastle (AW; from a similar mark) in January on his first attempt over 5f; less effective since, dwelling and failing to make his presence felt over C&D (tenth of 13; Class 3) latest; this race is easier and Paul Mulrennan is back aboard.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle (similar mark) in January on first attempt over 5f; less effective since.

runners.name: Highland Bear
runners.spotlight: Signs of ability in AW novice/maidens (5f-6f) but has looked something of a work in progress; taken out of her intended handicap debut at Wetherby (5.5f, good to firm) last week because of unsuitable ground, so this projected softer surface could suit; best watched for the moment.
runners.comment: Signs of ability in AW novice/maidens (5f-6f) but looked something of a work in progress.

runners.name: South Parade
runners.spotlight: 8lb lower than when last successful at Thirsk (5f Class 4, good to firm; 6-4 favourite) last August; struggled this season and reared at the start when in rear throughout (behind two of these) at Ripon latest.
runners.comment: Struggled this season and reared at the start when in rear throughout at Ripon latest
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Novak
runners.spotlight: On the go since the turn of the year but largely in good nick and he came second of nine in 7f Ayr handicap three weeks ago; must enter calculations off the same mark.
runners.comment: Good second over 7f at Ayr three weeks ago; must enter calculations off the same mark

runners.name: Ingleby Archie
runners.spotlight: Scored twice (5f/6f) last May and took a step forward with third of seven in 6f Catterick handicap 20 days ago; not out of things from an easing mark.
runners.comment: Scored twice last May; stepped forward with Catterick third 20 days ago; not out of things

runners.name: Jet Warrior
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who has thrived since sent handicapping, landing 6f handicaps at Ayr (good to firm) and Ripon (good) last month; another good run when third at Carlisle (6f, good to soft) last night; not taken at all lightly.
runners.comment: 6f wins at Ayr and Ripon in May; another good run when third at Carlisle last night

runners.name: No Return
runners.spotlight: A fair 6f scorer in Ireland last spring for Daniel Murphy; made a promising start for current yard with second at Lingfield (6f) in January but he's failed to build on it, a below-par fourth of seven back at Lingfield (6f) last time.
runners.comment: Arrives having been below par in recent starts, fourth of seven at Lingfield (6f) latest

runners.name: Iris Dancer
runners.spotlight: Course specialist; only sixth of nine on her first run of 2026 here over C&D six days ago but she took this prize 12 months ago, so no surprise if she bounces back; usually wears headgear but it is now removed.
runners.comment: Only sixth over C&D latest; took this prize 12 months ago, though, so can't be discounted

runners.name: Impressor
runners.spotlight: A three-time winner (including over C&D) here who got back on track when third of nine over C&D six days ago; goes well on soft ground, so this veteran is in the mix, especially if the rain comes.
runners.comment: C&D winner; back on track when C&D third latest; in the mix, especially if the rain comes

runners.name: Spirit Catcher
runners.spotlight: Returned to winning ways at Musselburgh (1m1f, good; from 4lb lower) in April and runner-up twice since, stamina seemingly overstretched behind the thriving Alpine Sierra at Musselburgh (12.5f) latest; enters calculations back over a more suitable 8.5f.
runners.comment: Stamina was seemingly overstretched behind thriving rival at Musselburgh (12.5f); chance.

runners.name: Mwafaq
runners.spotlight: Won reappearance, completing the hat-trick, at Thirsk (7f, good; from 4lb lower) in April; pressed the winner hard at Ayr (1m, good to firm) three weeks ago; this is a slightly longer trip; useful claimer in the saddle.
runners.comment: Pressed winner hard at Ayr (1m, good to firm) three weeks ago; useful claimer booked.
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Condotti
runners.spotlight: Still seeking first win (0-11) and now 10lb lower than when runner-up at Ffos Las (1m, good; 2-1 favourite) last August; weakened quickly when last of nine on reappearance at Carlisle (1m1f, good) 12 days ago.
runners.comment: Still seeking first win (0-11); weakened quickly on recent Carlisle reappearance (1m1f).

runners.name: Perfidia
runners.spotlight: Did enough to justify favouritism over C&D (good; from 2lb lower; Pisanello fourth) three back; held form reasonably well since, failing to get the breaks at Ripon (1m, good; fifth) last week; Jake Dickson's 7lb claim continues to be handy.
runners.comment: Did enough to justify favouritism over C&D (good; from 2lb lower) three back; consistent.

runners.name: South Road
runners.spotlight: Shock 6f winner at Ayr (good to soft; 40-1, from 5lb higher) last July; not much to enthuse about since (0-5) but she was returning from a two-month break when fifth at Ayr (1m, good to firm; Mwafaq runner-up) latest; stamina is a major risk over 8.5f.
runners.comment: Back from a break when fifth at Ayr latest (Mwafaq runner-up); stamina?

runners.name: Hunterian
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in four AW starts (at around a mile); took a slight step forward, showing speed, when third on turf debut at Redcar (7f, good to firm; 12-1) a fortnight ago; opposition suitable, but this return to 8.5f may not help; low mileage.
runners.comment: Slight step forward, showing speed, when third on recent turf debut at Redcar (7f; 12-1).

runners.name: Tees George
runners.spotlight: Fourth of 22 at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) in March and built on that when winning at Beverley (8.5f) the following month; latest Beverley effort was not so good and he has fared no better than sixth in six races from a mark of 61 and higher.
runners.comment: Beverley winner (8.5f) in May; latest effort not so good; possibly too high in weights.

runners.name: The Sweet Escape
runners.spotlight: Took advantage of a drop back in trip and ease in grade (into a Class 6) when scrambling home at Nottingham (8.5f, good; from 3lb lower) in April; beaten by a nose off this mark at Leicester (1m) in May and consistent since; possible.
runners.comment: Beaten by a nose off this mark at Leicester (1m) in May and consistent since; possible.

runners.name: Pisanello
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner; failed to score since York (1m1f, Class 4) two years ago; way down the weights now and 4l fourth to Perfidia over C&D (now 4lb lower and claimer ridden) on penultimate start, but usual tale of modest late headway continued at Carlisle last time; patience with him is wearing thin.
runners.comment: No win since York (1m1f Class 4) two years ago; patience with him is wearing thin.

runners.name: Millbuie
runners.spotlight: 25-race maiden; had run of the race at Newcastle (1m2f; third, 16-1) last week but was unable to make it count; no reason to think that this return to turf can unlock the door; others are more convincing.
runners.comment: 25-race maiden; had run of the race at Newcastle (1m2f; third) last week but still short.
runners.owner: N J Dalgarno & W R Ellis

runners.name: Motawaared
runners.spotlight: Placed in a novice and a maiden (both 1m) for George Boughey; low key (0-4, often slowly away) since joining Jim Goldie for 12,000gns; drops into a Class 6 for the first time, though, with cheekpieces added; no forlorn hope.
runners.comment: 0-4 for J Goldie; drops into Cl 6 for first time, with cheekpieces added; no forlorn hope

runners.name: Shushu
runners.spotlight: Solid start to her career when second of five behind a good prospect at Haydock three weeks ago (7f, soft); open to improvement but may not come into her own until tackling 1m+.
runners.comment: Encouraging 2nd on recent Haydock debut; may not really flourish until tackling 1m+

runners.name: Yimmna
runners.spotlight: 650,000euros yearling by Siyouni; second foal; half-sister to French 9.5f AW/1m2f turf winner Audubon Park (including Listed; RPR 112); dam placed 1m-9.5f in France, half-sister to French 1m4f Group 1 winner Left Hand; lots to like on paper and William Haggas won this race in 2022 (with a newcomer) and 2023; high on the list.
runners.comment: 650,000euros half-sister to Listed winner Audubon Park; yard won this race in 2022 & 2023

runners.name: Quadrillion
runners.spotlight: Top-class pedigree and she made a promising start to her career when third of six in a fillies' novice at Newmarket last July (7f, good to firm); odds-on favourite for a Leicester novice one month later when soundly beaten into second but the winner on that occasion is now BHA-rated 112 and finished fifth in the Irish 2000 Guineas; capable of better and she is a strong contender back from a break.
runners.comment: Beaten fav on both 2yo starts but bumped into a smart one when 2nd at Leicester; contender

runners.name: Too Too Divine
runners.spotlight: Modest form in her two 7f runs, the second of them over C&D six weeks ago (good to firm); might find life easier once handicapping.
runners.comment: Modest form in her two runs 177 days apart; handicaps more suitable after this

runners.name: Starlight Lass
runners.spotlight: By Dubawi; first foal of a 1m 2yo winner (RPR 90), herself a half-sister to St Leger winner Logician; stable won the corresponding event last year but with a filly who had experience; betting instructive.
runners.comment: Dam a well-connected 1m 2yo winner (RPR 90); betting useful; yard won this 12 months ago

runners.name: Sugar Sugar
runners.spotlight: Tenth foal; half-sister to five winners including The Night Porter (7f-1m2f including Hong Kong; RPR 95), Wonderful World (6f 2yo; 87) and Dancin Boy (1m-1m2f including Switzerland; 84); dam 7f winner (72), half-sister Irish 1,000 Guineas winner Samitar; apprentice-ridden and sports headgear on debut.
runners.comment: Good pedigree but apprentice-ridden and sports headgear on debut; best watched

runners.name: Esdaile
runners.spotlight: 15,000gns foal; by Oasis Dream; fifth foal; half-sister to 1m2f-1m4f winner Jawhary (RPR 82); dam unraced sister to 1m 2yo Group 3 winner Helleborine (dam of good sprinter Calyx) and 6f Group 1 winner African Rose; she has a useful standard to aim at and likely best watched.
runners.comment: 15,000gns half-sister to 1m2f-1m4f winner Jawhary (RPR 82); useful standard to aim at

runners.name: Kino Plasmat
