runners.spotlight: 250,000gns foal; eighth foal; Wootton Basset half-sister to winners Waikuku (7f-1m1f including multiple HK Group 1s; RPR 124), Waipiro (1m2f including Group 3; 113), It's Good To Laugh (7.3f 2yo/1m4f Flat, 99; 2m-2m3f hurdle) and Dancing Rebel (5f 2yo; 84); dam placed 7f (72); lots to like on paper and he is one to note for market confidence.
runners.comment: 250,000gns foal; half-sister to four winners, two of whom are smart; betting instructive

runners.name: La Mariposa
runners.spotlight: 40-1 when a tailed-off last of five on her Newmarket debut eight weeks ago (7f, good); longer-term prospect.
runners.comment: Little short-term promise on April's Newmarket debut (7f, good)
runners.owner: L J Vaessen And Stephen Barber

runners.name: Victorian Rose
runners.spotlight: 80-1 and well beaten on Kempton debut three weeks ago (7f); hard to recommend.
runners.comment: 80-1 and well beaten on Kempton debut three weeks ago (7f); hard to recommend

runners.name: Sterling Fortune
runners.spotlight: Never the slightest threat on her Kempton debut five weeks ago (7f, AW) but only one of the other 13 runners covered the final furlong faster; should do better at some point.
runners.comment: No threat to the principals on last month's debut but there was promise in the run

runners.name: Wild Rosie
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden; faded into sixth of 13 in first-time cheekpieces at Newmarket (1m) most recently; bred to do better still, being related to two winners for her connections; possibilities dropped back in trip.
runners.comment: Faded over 1m last time; bred to do better still; possibilities back down in trip

runners.name: White Ladder
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; belied his 40-1 odds with 1l third upped to 7f at Salisbury (good) on stable/seasonal debut; possibilities off same mark back down in grade, provided he builds on that effort.
runners.comment: Good third upped to 7f at Salisbury on stable/seasonal debut; possibilities off same mark

runners.name: Jungle Knight
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; shaped a bit better than the bare result, while seeming outstayed over 1m, at Haydock (good to soft) most recently; could go well off a reduced mark back down in trip.
runners.comment: Shaped a bit better than bare result, while seeming outstayed over 1m, last time

runners.name: Superstorm
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden whose consistent 7f form includes making the frame in all three outings this term, latest at Lingfield (good) returned to turf from AW; solid contender who is in the mix.
runners.comment: Consistent 7f form includes making the frame in all three runs this term; solid chance

runners.name: Miss Starlet
runners.spotlight: Six-race career can be split into two contrasting halves, namely steady progress in maidens and steady regress in handicaps; hence a turnaround is required.
runners.comment: Made steady progress in maidens but has steadily regressed in handicaps

runners.name: Blindfold Games
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who has made the frame a few times over sprint trips on AW; failed to transfer that peak form back to turf (6f) last time; enough to prove back up in distance.
runners.comment: Failed to transfer best AW form back to turf last time; something to prove

runners.name: Inclusive
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent maiden who has been beaten about 8l in each of her two starts for this yard; first-time cheekpieces need to make a difference.
runners.comment: First-time cheekpieces need to make a difference

runners.name: Blossom In The Air
runners.spotlight: Showed no improvement at Lingfield (6f) on stable/handicap/seasonal debut and remains far from solid in form terms.
runners.comment: Far from solid on form

runners.name: Pennine Way
runners.spotlight: Related to several winners (mostly useful/smart) for her connections; has shown promise at maiden/novice level and looks likely to improve now handicapping with Catterick reappearance under her belt; interesting.
runners.comment: Related to winners for her connections and should improve now handicapping; interesting

runners.name: Komodo Rose
runners.spotlight: Form in 7f AW maidens comprises two efforts in the frame last term for previous yard and a reappearance run that appeared to be needed; has modest RPRs but may show progress now handicapping.
runners.comment: Has modest RPRs but may show progress now handicapping on turf

runners.name: Molly Mac
runners.spotlight: Placed in four of her last five 2yo starts but seems rather exposed and is the only runner in this field who lacks recent match practice; makes stable/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Rather exposed and lacks recent match practice; stable/seasonal debut

runners.name: Accommodation
runners.spotlight: Modest third in final AW attempt and ran dismally (last of 14) upped to 1m at Windsor on handicap/turf debut since; best watched unless the market suggests a bigger effort is likely.
runners.comment: Ran dismally on handicap/turf debut last time

runners.name: Jooliah
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden who has a poor chance on bare figures achieved mostly over 6f; gives 7f another go.
runners.comment: Poor chance on bare figures

runners.name: Bear Steps
runners.spotlight: Showed no improvement switched to handicap level last time, finishing midfield, and still has RPRs in the 30s; chance depends on the effects of first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Chance depends on the effects of first-time headgear

runners.name: Albert Cee
runners.spotlight: Didn't get home over 7f at Southwell in March and appreciated the drop to 6f when winning there 20 days ago following a short break; both wins on turf have been on good to soft, and has claims off this 3lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Both wins on turf have been on good to soft; claims up 3lb for his Southwell AW success

runners.name: Kyoto
runners.spotlight: 0-4 but did shape with some encouragement at Southwell (6f, AW) on his handicap debut 20 days ago after being held up from his wide stall; makes his turf debut and he's open to progress.
runners.comment: 0-4 (AW) but did shape with some promise on his h'cap debut at Southwell; open to progress

runners.name: Skellig Isle
runners.spotlight: Unraced on soft; came from well back to win at Chelmsford (5f, AW) on her stable debut in March and bounced back from a lesser effort when fourth of six at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) last month; however, she was a bit below her best on turf 16 days ago; stable won this last year.
runners.comment: Unraced on soft; made a winning stable debut on the AW; slightly below her best last time

runners.name: Court Drive
runners.spotlight: Sole win on turf came on good to firm at Pontefract last August off 4lb higher; had to switch for a run and couldn't finish strongly enough when last of six at Wolverhampton 36 days ago but shaped as if in form; however, others make a bit more appeal.
runners.comment: Sole turf win came on good to firm; shaped as if in form when last of six at Pontefract

runners.name: Mart
runners.spotlight: Seven wins from 47 starts, the most recent success coming at Lingfield (6f, good) last May off an 11lb higher mark; respectable effort when fourth of 12 at Redcar (6f, good to firm) 15 days ago and now needs to build on that.
runners.comment: 11lb below his last winning mark and ran respectably last time; needs to build on that

runners.name: Elvetham
runners.spotlight: Unproven on ground slower than good; justified market support when winning in first-time blinkers at Lingfield (6f, AW) in April and has finished third in each of his three starts since, most recently at Thirsk (6f, good) five days ago; solid chance if handling conditions.
runners.comment: Third in three starts since winning at Lingfield (AW); unproven on ground slower than good

runners.name: Court Of Session
runners.spotlight: Five-time AW winner who belatedly opened his account on turf when coming from off the pace to win at Nottingham (6f, good) a week ago; has form on ground slower than good and has to be respected under a 4lb penalty.
runners.comment: Opened his account on turf when winning at Nottingham last week; respected under a penalty

runners.name: Big Bard
runners.spotlight: Two of his five wins on turf have been on soft; came back to form when winning at Kempton (6f, AW) in January but he's been well beaten in both starts since; a 63-day break needs to have done him good.
runners.comment: Won a Kempton in January but well beaten in both starts since; absent for 63 days

runners.name: Alafdhal
runners.spotlight: Last five wins have been over 5f, the most recent at Wolverhampton (AW) in December; below his best in four starts since and now returns to turf for the first time since October 2024 following a 120-day break; best form on grass has been on soft/heavy.
runners.comment: Below form when last seen but best turf form on soft/heavy; last five wins over 5f

runners.name: Star Of Jupiter
runners.spotlight: 1-19, with the win coming in a classified event at Wolverhampton (9.5f, AW; first-time blinkers) last February; however, he has been in the first three on six occasions, including on turf on a variety of ground; returns to turf with place claims.
runners.comment: Sole win came at Wolverhampton but placed five times on turf; holds place claims

runners.name: Cloudy Rose
runners.spotlight: Had 1m4f and 1m6f wins on consecutive starts last summer and she's now 4lb below her last winning mark; couldn't reach the leaders when fifth of ten at Bath (1m6f; good to firm) 19 days ago but she won't mind this slower ground and could go well.
runners.comment: Won over 1m4f and 1m6f last summer; 4lb below her last winning mark and could go well

runners.name: Argentum
runners.spotlight: 0-13 but she has posted some solid efforts, including when third of 12 at Kempton (1m4f, AW) in January; respectable effort there in first-time cheekpieces three weeks ago but her stamina for this far isn't assured.
runners.comment: Posted some solid efforts, but now 0-13 and her stamina for this trip isn't assured

runners.name: Urban Warrior
runners.spotlight: Didn't get home but did show promise when fourth of 10 at Wolverhampton (1m6f, AW; first-time hood) in January; posted a solid effort here over 1m2f (good) three weeks ago but this step back up in trip isn't sure to suit.
runners.comment: 0-5; posted a solid effort here over 1m2f but step back up in trip isn't sure to suit
runners.owner: W Enticknap, B Ralph And Partner

runners.name: Captain Robert
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 14 starts came on the AW at Chelmsford (1m6f) in December but he's posted some good efforts since, including on turf at Catterick (2m, good to firm) 11 days ago; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Posted some good efforts since winning at Chelmsford, including on turf latest; e-w chance

runners.name: Beggarman
runners.spotlight: Both wins on turf have been at Ffos Las (1m6f/2m; good to soft/good); now 2lb lower than that latest success and returned from a break with a solid effort at Southwell (2m, AW) 34 days ago; claims.
runners.comment: 2lb below his last winning mark and returned from a break with a solid effort on the AW
runners.owner: Mrs E Bickerton And A Streeter

runners.name: Smokey Malone
runners.spotlight: 2-24 on turf compared to 9-43 on the AW; completed an AW hat-trick when winning at Chelmsford (1m6f) in February off a 2lb lower mark, but he was too free over 2m at Lingfield (good) 23 days ago; dropping back in trip should suit.
runners.comment: Too free over 2m at Lingfield (turf) 23 days ago but dropping back in trip should suit

runners.name: Dissident
runners.spotlight: Hurdle winner at Doncaster (2m, good to soft; first-time blinkers) in December but struggled in his next five starts; ran a bit better when sixth of nine at Bath (1m2f, firm) 15 days ago but isn't certain to back that up; cheekpieces replace blinkers.
runners.comment: Hurdle winner at Doncaster in December; ran a bit better last time after some poor runs

runners.name: High Favour
runners.spotlight: 0-3 for Jim Bolger and he's been well beaten in four starts on the AW for this stable, including in a first-time hood at Lingfield 75 days ago; switches back to turf having been off since.
runners.comment: 0-3 in Ireland and struggled in four starts on the AW for this stable; off for 75 days

runners.name: Gorgeous Mr George
runners.spotlight: Won three times here last year (good to soft/good to firm), the most recent coming over C&D off a 10lb lower mark than today's; eighth in both starts since returning from a 182-day break and better is needed.
runners.comment: Won here three times last year; eighth in both starts since return; better is needed

runners.name: Where's Freddy
runners.spotlight: All three wins on turf have been on the Newmarket July course (7f/1m) but he has been placed in two of his three starts over this C&D, including on soft; never featured on his reappearance 25 days ago and needs to build on that.
runners.comment: All three turf wins on the Newmarket July course; never featured on his reappearance

runners.name: Sprightly Dance
runners.spotlight: Made a promising debut here on good ground in October and built on that when winning comfortably at Wolverhampton (9.5f, AW) next time; disappointing at Chelmsford (1m2f, AW) 196 days ago, weakening late after racing freely, but she may settle better over this shorter trip; interesting on her handicap debut.
runners.comment: Disappointing over 1m2f at Chelmsford 196 days ago; may settle better over this trip

runners.name: Cavolo Nero
runners.spotlight: Won twice as a 3yo, including a handicap at Goodwood (1m, good) last May; placed on his first two starts this year (1m/1m2f) but below his best when held in fourth at Haydock (1m2f, good to firm) 46 days ago; drops back in trip with blinkers now tried.
runners.comment: Placed on his first two starts this year but below his best latest; blinkers now tried

runners.name: Bella Perla
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden at Southwell (7f, AW) and two 1m handicaps at Lingfield (AW) last year when trained by the Crisfords; drops in grade having failed to beat a rival home on her stable debut at Ascot (1m, good) 32 days ago and has a bit to prove on turf.
runners.comment: Won three times on the AW for the Crisford stable; well beaten at Ascot on yard debut
runners.owner: Mr And Mrs P Whight And T Clover

runners.name: Renewal
runners.spotlight: Record of 4232 in novice/maiden events and ran well when second of 12 on her handicap debut at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) in October; however, she failed to beat a rival home at Thirsk (1m) on her reappearance and needs to take a big step forward from that effort.
runners.comment: Runner-up on her handicap debut in October but didn't beat a rival on her reappearance

runners.name: Carron
runners.spotlight: Made it 3-4 on the AW when winning at Newcastle (1m, AW) in March but below that level in two starts on turf since, including when last of his group of five at Newmarket (1m; first-time cheekpieces) 39 days ago; cheekpieces discarded.
runners.comment: 3-4 on the AW compared to 1-10 on turf; below his best on last two starts; cheekpieces off

runners.name: Advancing
runners.spotlight: All three of his wins have been on the AW, the most recent at Kempton (1m) in March off 4lb lower; never a threat when sixth at Windsor (1m, good to firm) 16 days ago and he was well beaten on soft ground last year.
runners.comment: All three wins on the AW, the most recent off 4lb lower; never a threat at Windsor latest

runners.name: Percival
runners.spotlight: Heavy-ground winner over 1m2f at Saint-Cloud in November 2024 but tailed off in a 1m4f handicap there on his final start for Andre Fabre 87 days ago; has since been purchased for 12,000gns and drops to 1m with a hood applied on his stable debut; watch the market.
runners.comment: Tailed off on final start for Andre Fabre; check the market on his stable debut; hooded

runners.name: Padua
runners.spotlight: Can race freely, just as he did when making all at Haydock (7f, good) last summer; didn't get away with it at Sandown (7f, good) 307 days ago and he's since been gelded; tongue-tie is removed and he needs to settling better now stepping back up in trip on his reappearance.
runners.comment: Made all at Haydock (7f) last summer; needs to settle better returned to 1m; gelded
runners.owner: P C J Dalby & R D Schuster

runners.name: Mythical Guest
runners.spotlight: Disappointing on reappearance at Newmarket (1m, good; reported to have stopped quickly) 57 days ago and both wins came over 1m2f on good to firm in 2024 (one here); however, he did post some good efforts last season (including on soft) and may be able to put his comeback run well behind him.
runners.comment: Disappointing on his reappearance but posted some good efforts last year and acts on soft

runners.name: Very Demure
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden who is interesting on her penultimate effort, namely good second in C&D handicap when wearing first-time cheekpieces and just in front of Lady Of Clover on worse terms; now blinkered.
runners.comment: Interesting on C&D effort two starts ago when just in front of Lady Of Clover

runners.name: Heer's Sadie
runners.spotlight: Three-time course winner, including last June/July; could go well returned to this venue with Lingfield reappearance under her belt.
runners.comment: Three-time course winner, including last June/July; could go well returned to this venue

runners.name: Contemplation
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden whose last two efforts (1m2f) suggest this return to 1m will suit; otherwise has weak claims on overall form.
runners.comment: Return to 1m is likely to suit; otherwise has weak claims on overall from

runners.name: Lady Of Clover
runners.spotlight: In-form 3yo who ran well over C&D on penultimate start, then opened her account in Wolverhampton classified contest (8.5f, AW) last week; may improve further at this level; respected even with 6lb penalty.
runners.comment: Ran well over C&D on penultimate start; won AW classified event last week; respected

runners.name: Girls Allowed
runners.spotlight: Showed improvement with fourth-place finish at Leicester (7f) on handicap/seasonal debut; possibilities if building on that effort.
runners.comment: Possibilities if building on Leicester reappearance effort (finished fourth)

runners.name: Nicely Curved
runners.spotlight: Maiden whose Flat form for current yard comprises contrasting efforts in two Lingfield AW classified events; not sure what to expect.
runners.comment: Maiden; Flat form for current yard comprises contrasting efforts in AW classified races

runners.name: Lowestoft
runners.spotlight: Improved AW effort when fourth in Southwell handicap (7f) on stable debut last time; needs to prove he can transfer the form to turf.
runners.comment: Needs to prove he can transfer latest AW effort (stable debut) back to turf

runners.name: Caitlin G
runners.spotlight: Poor claims on her form over shorter; combination of new trip and first-time hood needs to make a difference.
runners.comment: Combination of new trip and hood needs to make a difference

runners.name: Harry Don't Bite
runners.spotlight: Not particularly solid on his AW form (five starts); chance largely depends on the effects of this switch to turf.
runners.comment: Not particularly solid on his AW form

runners.name: Rockafeller Skank
runners.spotlight: Finished last in three runs last autumn; gelded since; the market signals may help to ascertain whether improvement is likely.
runners.comment: Finished last in three runs last autumn; gelded since

runners.name: Viscountess Nelson
runners.spotlight: Won Irish maiden point in April last year; stepped up from her hurdles debut to open her account in this sphere at Perth (2m4f, good) in April; carries a 7lb penalty but is the one to beat.
runners.comment: Stepped up from her hurdles debut to open her account in this sphere at Perth in April.
runners.owner: Old Gold Racing With Francesca Cumani

runners.name: Elated
runners.spotlight: Promising efforts when finishing third in her two runs over hurdles, over 2m at Chepstow and Warwick; looks sure to be suited by this step up in trip and shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Promising efforts when finishing third in her two runs over hurdles, over 2m.

runners.name: Evening Tess
runners.spotlight: Showing steady improvement with each run, latest when runner-up on her handicap debut at Newton Abbot (2m2f, good) four weeks ago; looks sure to be suited by this step up in trip and holds claims.
runners.comment: Runner-up on her handicap debut at Newton Abbot four weeks ago.

runners.name: Frenati
runners.spotlight: Landed bumper at Stratford (2m, soft) in March; fell at the first flight on her hurdles debut at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good; 4-1) two weeks ago; steps up in trip and should run well provided that fall hasn't left a mark.
runners.comment: Fell at the first flight on her hurdles debut at Newton Abbot two weeks ago.

runners.name: My Lady d'Ex
runners.spotlight: Beaten 19l and 33l in her two bumpers; steps up in trip on her hurdles debut and needs to show significant improvement.
runners.comment: Beaten 19l and 33l in her two bumpers; steps up in trip on her hurdles debut.

runners.name: Maria Mes
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in her two runs in bumpers; steps up in trip on her hurdles debut but probably best watched until she shows more.
runners.comment: Well beaten in her two runs in bumpers; steps up in trip on her hurdles debut.

runners.name: A Little Something
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark on her 13th start under rules, and her fourth attempt over fences, when accounting for two rivals (only one other completed) at Stratford (2m3f, good to firm) last month; runs off the same mark and this is tougher but still holds claims.
runners.comment: Got off the mark on her 13th start under rules at Stratford last month.

runners.name: Gore Point
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner and won two hurdles races at Newton Abbot last summer; best of three runs over fences when runner-up at Taunton (2m2f, good to firm) in November when last seen; should go close if he is ready to roll.
runners.comment: Best of three runs over fences when runner-up at Taunton in November when last seen.

runners.name: Wellwillya
runners.spotlight: Second of two wins over fences came at this track (2m5f, heavy) in February 2025; comfortably held when fourth of seven finishers over C&D (good) last month and needs to show improvement.
runners.comment: Comfortably held when fourth over C&D last month and needs to show improvement.

runners.name: Atreides
runners.spotlight: Consistent sort who was successful in first two attempts in visor, in December, including over C&D (heavy); should have benefitted from his first run for four months when sixth of nine at Worcester (2m4f, good) last month; visor returns to replace cheekpieces and won't be far away.
runners.comment: Consistent sort whose second of two wins over fences, in December, came over C&D.

runners.name: Doctor Glide
runners.spotlight: Showed little in four runs over fences when trained in Ireland, or in three runs over hurdles for current yard; won a couple of points over 2m4f last month but this is a different ball game on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Won a couple of points last month but this is a different ball game on handicap debut.

runners.name: Almuhit
runners.spotlight: Both wins over hurdles in February/March 2025 when trained by Gary Brown; has raced mainly on the Flat for current yard, winning twice at Newbury last summer; fourth in valuable 24-runner handicap hurdle at Punchestown (2m3f, yielding) in April; should go close if running to form.
runners.comment: Fourth in valuable 24-runner handicap hurdle at Punchestown in April; should go close.

runners.name: Sun Joy
runners.spotlight: Dual winning hurdler over about 2m5f; beaten 17l at this track (2m3f) last month following a six-month break and not sure that the drop back in trip will be in his favour.
runners.comment: Beaten 17l at this track (2m3f) last month following a six-month break.

runners.name: Break Point
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in a Perth maiden hurdle in August last year when trained by Olly Murphy; last of ten finishers at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good) on first hurdles start for current trainer 17 days ago; needs to return to form to figure.
runners.comment: Last of ten finishers at Uttoxeter on first hurdles start for current trainer 17 days ago.

runners.name: Ballyfinn
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark on his fifth attempt over fences, over C&D (good) last month; nowhere near that level here (2m3f, good) eight days later but maybe he can return to form back to his winning distance.
runners.comment: Won over fences over C&D last month; nowhere near that level here eight days later.

runners.name: Edgewell
runners.spotlight: Won twice at Hereford over 3m1f in the spring but has found life tougher since; pulled up at Warwick (3m1f, good) three weeks ago; others preferred.
runners.comment: Won twice at Hereford over 3m1f in the spring but has found life tougher since.

runners.name: Special John
runners.spotlight: 3-5 over fences, his latest success at Bangor (3m, good) last month a career-best; needs to step up again in this better contest but going the right way.
runners.comment: 3-5 over fences, his latest success at Bangor last month a career-best.

runners.name: Gata Ban
runners.spotlight: No match for Ballyfinn when having to settle for second place over C&D (good) last month, but opened her account over fences at Worcester (2m7f, good) on Saturday; shoulders a 7lb penalty and this looks tougher.
runners.comment: Second to Ballyfinn over C&D last month; first win over fences, at Worcester on Saturday.

runners.name: Kylenoe Dancer
runners.spotlight: Sole success came over C&D in May last year; seemed to need first run back from ten-month absence and fared better when third of seven at Worcester (2m4f, good) 19 days ago; should be spot on now and holds claims.
runners.comment: C&D winner last May; ran encouragingly at Worcester last time; leading player.

runners.name: A Tickatickatiming
runners.spotlight: Sole success over hurdles came in a Plumpton maiden in November 2023; has shown bits of form since but was pulled up at Plumpton (2m, good) when last seen; difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Sole success over hurdles came in a Plumpton maiden in November 2023.

runners.name: Easter Icon
runners.spotlight: Has six wins on the Flat; sole success in this sphere came at this track (2m5f, good) in August 2024; still 6lb above his winning mark but was still in contention when brought down at the last flight over C&D (good) in April; holds place claims.
runners.comment: Still in contention when brought down at the last flight over C&D in April.

runners.name: Galactic Jack
runners.spotlight: Useful on the Flat in his prime; yet to win over hurdles, but has made the frame on his last three starts, since returning from a lengthy absence; down to a career-low mark and holds claims.
runners.comment: Yet to win over hurdles but has made the frame on his last three starts.

runners.name: Kates Choice
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark under rules at the 13th attempt at Ffos Las (2m4f, good to soft) three weeks ago; 4lb rise isn't harsh and she should be competitive.
runners.comment: Got off the mark under rules at the 13th attempt at Ffos Las three weeks ago.

runners.name: Mistress Fox
runners.spotlight: Is gradually getting the hang of things over hurdles, running up to her best when runner-up here (2m5f, good) last month; should not be far away.
runners.comment: Ran up to her best when runner-up here last month.

runners.name: Dunaden Island
runners.spotlight: 1-9 in points; has shown little in four starts over hurdles; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: 1-9 in points; has shown little in four starts over hurdles; hard to fancy.

runners.name: Charles Morin
runners.spotlight: Hasn't raced much over hurdles and shaped as though this step up in trip will suit when staying on into fourth at Plumpton (2m, good to soft) in March; beaten in low-grade contests on Flat since; place claims.
runners.comment: Shaped as though this step up in trip will suit when fourth at Plumpton in March.

runners.name: Maestrow
runners.spotlight: Has shown little so far; steps up in trip on handicap debut; major yard; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Has shown little so far; steps up in trip on handicap debut; major yard.

runners.name: Our Brother John
runners.spotlight: Achieved little in three runs under rules in 2023; has been running in points since but last win in that sphere was also in 2023; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Achieved little in three runs under rules in 2023; has been running in points since.

runners.name: Muskerry Rock
runners.spotlight: Dual point winner who came good under rules with front-running success in Wexford hunter chase (2m7f, good to yielding; refitted cheekpieces) last month; changed hands subsequently for £17,000 and has to be over interest back over hurdles for top new yard.
runners.comment: Made all in hunter chase last month; subsequently sold and back over hurdles for top yard.

runners.name: Jack The Savage
runners.spotlight: Landed a weak handicap at Hereford (3m1f, good) in March; never threatened off 2lb higher at Southwell (3m, good) last time; Sean Bowen rides for the first time so may well have a say.
runners.comment: Landed a weak handicap at Hereford in March; fourth off 2lb higher at Southwell last time.

runners.name: Alan Bresil
runners.spotlight: Has achieved little over hurdles; second of two finishers at Huntingdon (3m1f, good to firm) 16 days ago.
runners.comment: Has achieved little over hurdles; second of two finishers at Huntingdon 16 days ago.

runners.name: Fravanco
runners.spotlight: 0-5 over hurdles but seemed suited by the new trip when runner-up over C&D (good) in April; should not be far away.
runners.comment: 0-5 over hurdles but seemed suited by the new trip when runner-up over C&D in April.

runners.name: Good Girl Rachel
runners.spotlight: Has shown only modest form over hurdles, including on handicap debut when fourth of eight at Ffos Las (3m, good to soft) three weeks ago in first-time cheekpieces which are replaced by visor here; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Has shown only modest form, latest when fourth at Ffos Las three weeks ago.

runners.name: Smiling Emperor
runners.spotlight: On a losing run of 11 and hasn't been overly threatening in those races; will do well to be in the mix from a wide draw.
runners.comment: On a losing run of 11 and hasn't been overly threatening in those races

runners.name: Dragon Sunrise
runners.spotlight: Nearly shed his maiden tag over 1m here in March but he'll need to better his subsequent efforts when strong in the betting.
runners.comment: Near miss here in March but he'll need to better his subsequent efforts
runners.owner: Sunny Wong Chi Tat & Wong Tsz Wah

runners.name: Smart Beauty
runners.spotlight: Reserve; has only won at Sha Tin and was beaten over 5l into third here two weeks ago (seemed to stay the 1m3f); has to step up.
runners.comment: Reserve; has only won at Sha Tin but was beaten over 5l into third here two weeks ago

runners.name: Mr Aladdin
runners.spotlight: Now 0-35 and held by some of these on their runs here a fortnight ago; out of the weights.
runners.comment: Now 0-35 and held by some of these on their runs here a fortnight ago

runners.name: Kolachi
runners.spotlight: Close fourth here in March but disappointing since, though did have a wide draw when dropped into a Class 5 last time at Sha Tin.
runners.comment: Did have a wide draw when dropped into a Class 5 last time at Sha Tin

runners.name: Gold Tack
runners.spotlight: Just 1-38 and wouldn't be the obvious answer in his current form.
runners.comment: Just 1-38 and wouldn't be the obvious answer in his current form
runners.owner: Robert Chung Wai Shu & Jeanette Chung Siu Wah

runners.name: Smart City
runners.spotlight: His second poor run on dirt last time so can be forgiven that; respected on a number of good runs around here and he's on a handy mark.
runners.comment: Dirt an excuse last time; respected on a number of good runs around here

runners.name: Smart Trio
runners.spotlight: Reserve; won races in Australia but very opposable on his exploits in Hong Kong.
runners.comment: Reserve; won races in Australia but very opposable on his exploits in Hong Kong
runners.owner: Peter Lee Kwok Wah, Tony Lau Hon Kwan & Kenny Cheng Tsin Ki

runners.name: Perfect Pairing
runners.spotlight: Poor strike-rate but could easily have won more than he has and another good effort here two weeks ago over 1m3f.
runners.comment: Could have won more than he has and another good effort here two weeks ago over 1m3f
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Jerry Chow Shiu Ming

runners.name: All Are Mine
runners.spotlight: Behind some of these here two weeks ago when perhaps finding 1m3f beyond him; has twice gone close over this trip in recent weeks.
runners.comment: 1m3f a stretch last time; has twice gone close over this trip in recent weeks

runners.name: Kingly Demeanor
runners.spotlight: Twice runner-up here in the spring and slower surfaces perhaps not ideal on last two outings at Sha Tin; has been contesting Class 4s as well so from a good draw he's firmly in the reckoning with Britney Wong taking off 7lb.
runners.comment: Class dropper who will appreciate the return to this track and faster ground

runners.name: Splendid Force
runners.spotlight: Better when runner-up over C&D in April and was stopped in his tracks when upped to 1m3f two weeks ago; possible.
runners.comment: Runner-up over C&D in April and was stopped in his tracks when upped to 1m3f
runners.owner: Colin Chu Wan Kin & Mandy Luo Wen

runners.name: Lucky Blessing
runners.spotlight: Made all over the 1m here in November but hasn't approached that form since; others preferred.
runners.comment: Made all over the 1m here in November but hasn't approached that form since

runners.name: Kyrus Treasure
runners.spotlight: Yet to make the frame in nine races and wouldn't be the obvious answer despite dropping in grade.
runners.comment: Unplaced all nine races and wouldn't be the obvious answer despite dropping in grade

runners.name: Legend Winner
runners.spotlight: Won all three races in Australia; 0-8 in Hong Kong but appreciated the drop in grade when a length third over C&D two weeks ago, though was better drawn then than today; still considered with Zac Purton taking over.
runners.comment: Drawn wide but appreciated the drop in grade when a length third over C&D

runners.name: Our Lucky Glory
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner here at 6f; losing run is mounting up but he stays this far and hasn't been running too badly, latterly in the new cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Three-time winner here at 6f; losing run is mounting up but he stays this far

runners.name: Very Grateful
runners.spotlight: Successful in New Zealand but it's proving a struggle in Hong Kong and well held in his last three starts; failed to convince on his first run over 1m here last time.
runners.comment: Successful in New Zealand but it's proving a struggle in Hong Kong

runners.name: To Infinity
runners.spotlight: Multiple C&D winner but hasn't been firing for a while now and reservations remain.
runners.comment: Multiple C&D winner but hasn't been firing for a while now and reservations remain
runners.owner: Chan Wing Chiu & Yildiz Choi Yat Fan

runners.name: Gratifide
runners.spotlight: Reserve; won in Australia but has made a disappointing start to his Hong Kong career.
runners.comment: Reserve; won in Australia but has made a disappointing start to his Hong Kong career
runners.owner: Wong Leung Sau Hing & Eddie Wong Ming Chak

runners.name: Noble Pursuit
runners.spotlight: Three course wins, two over this trip; not beaten far into fifth here last time and has each-way claims off a handy mark.
runners.comment: Three course wins, two over this trip; not beaten far into fifth here last time

runners.name: Precision Hope
runners.spotlight: Hasn't had a lot of racing and was bang there off this career-low mark over C&D four weeks ago; firmly in calculations from stall 1.
runners.comment: Hasn't had a lot of racing and was bang there off this career-low mark over C&D latest

runners.name: Viva Boss
runners.spotlight: Not that competitive in any of his six races and can be taken on for now.
runners.comment: Not that competitive in any of his six races and can be taken on for now

runners.name: Good Luck Win
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner but 0-14 since the last of those well over a year ago; recent form underwhelming.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner but 0-14 since the last of those well over a year ago
runners.owner: Siu Wai Chung & Lo Nga Yin

runners.name: Sure Joyful
runners.spotlight: Had lost his way but he was only beaten a short head and a nose into third here two runs back and a wide draw did him for three weeks ago; stall 9 isn't ideal today but he should have some sort of chance.
runners.comment: Chance on penultimate effort and drawn wide last time; not that stall 9 is great
runners.owner: Star Chan Iu Seng, Simon Chan Cham Man Et Al

runners.name: Cheaha
runners.spotlight: A winner in New Zealand; only his second run at this lower level when a closing fourth over 6f here recently; unproven beyond that far and he's got a wide draw.
runners.comment: Closing fourth here recently but over 6f and he's unproven beyond that far

runners.name: Superb Kid
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been over 7f at Sha Tin where he's done the vast majority of his racing; no better than seventh in two visits here and form figures at 1m read 0785070.
runners.comment: No better than seventh in two visits here and form figures at 1m read 0785070

runners.name: Leslie
runners.spotlight: Reserve; this year's wins have been on dirt (1m; slowly away on that surface last time); capable on turf but looks on a tough mark.
runners.comment: Reserve; this year's wins have been on dirt; capable on turf but looks on a tough mark

runners.name: Silver Spurs
runners.spotlight: Won over 5f and 6f here last year and ran a cracker from stall 14 when runner-up at Sha Tin (6f) recently; has another tough draw to deal with but has to rate a possible.
runners.comment: Won over 5f/6f here last year and ran a cracker from stall 14 when runner-up at Sha Tin

runners.name: Golden Friendship
runners.spotlight: When second here in April he was no match for the winner; may need Class 5s to regain the winning thread.
runners.comment: Well beaten by the winner two runs ago; may need Class 5s to regain the winning thread
runners.owner: Janet Wong Hoi Shan, John Yuen Se Kit Et Al

runners.name: King Oberon
runners.spotlight: Edging back down to a favourable mark but seems to be going through the motions right now; not overly appealing despite the presence of Purton.
runners.comment: Edging back down to a favourable mark but seems to be going through the motions right now

runners.name: Jolly Companion
runners.spotlight: Dropping back down the weights and has the form to feature off this mark; very effective at 6f and around here.
runners.comment: Dropping back down the weights and has the form to feature off this mark

runners.name: Sunny Da Best
runners.spotlight: 6f/7f wins at Sha Tin were a good while back now but he continues to run well now and again; down in grade off a reduced mark; couldn't discount for all that he's drawn wide.
runners.comment: Runs well now and again; down in grade off a reduced mark; couldn't discount

runners.name: Holmes A Court
runners.spotlight: Group 3 winner in Australia; not disgraced here in two visits during March and new trainer brings blinkers back into play; has done nothing in his recent trials but he's of some interest.
runners.comment: Not disgraced here in two visits during March and new trainer reapplies the blinkers

runners.name: Rising Elite
runners.spotlight: First worthwhile form when beaten just over 2l into fifth over this far at Sha Tin and might be on the upgrade.
runners.comment: First worthwhile form when beaten just over 2l into fifth over this far at Sha Tin

runners.name: Amazing Victory
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last June after which he was off for 273 days; back to form when second here four weeks ago, though he raced prominently from a good draw and now he's out in 11.
runners.comment: Second here recently; raced prominently from a good draw and now he's out in 11

runners.name: Diamond Sparkle
runners.spotlight: Reserve; ran okay over 6f to start with and latest backward step was at 7f; others much safer, however.
runners.comment: Reserve; ran okay over 6f to start with and latest backward step was at 7f
runners.owner: Lau So Wah & Donna Ng Siu Ying

runners.name: Heroic Vanguard
runners.spotlight: There was promise in his debut at Sha Tin and again here three weeks ago as he was beaten under 3l from a bad draw; now in stall 3 and not yet exposed.
runners.comment: Debut promise and here last time when not beaten too far from a bad draw

runners.name: Bullish Nova
runners.spotlight: Third in a trial before filling the same berth on C&D debut two weeks ago; he was beaten 5l but he got well behind before running on takingly; open to plenty of improvement.
runners.comment: Beaten 5l into third on debut here but got well behind before running on takingly

runners.name: Word Of Kindness
runners.spotlight: Had three official races, the standout effort his close fourth over C&D; had a wide trip when last at Sha Tin a month ago; recent trial efforts were underwhelming but he could have a big run in him.
runners.comment: Had three official races, the standout effort his close fourth over C&D; wide trip latest

runners.name: Mega Awesome
runners.spotlight: Well held in two 6f handicaps at Sha Tin and cheekpieces need to bring him on.
runners.comment: Well held in two 6f handicaps at Sha Tin and cheekpieces need to bring him on
runners.owner: Joyce Lee & Alan Kiang Ping Fai

runners.name: Superb Spirit
runners.spotlight: Reserve; has done well in trials; encouraging fourth on debut and then when again favourite, he did it cosily under Zac Purton back at Sha Tin; he's promising and would be a strong contender if getting a run.
runners.comment: Reserve; fourth then first, both times under Purton; strong contender if getting a run

runners.name: Jumbo Blessing
runners.spotlight: Failed to fire in two of his five races but the other three were highly encouraging, last time finishing close up under Moreira from a high draw; again out wide today but there are races in him off this mark.
runners.comment: Has again dropped unfortunate with the draw but there are races in him off this mark
runners.owner: Billy Chan Wing Kwong, Kiki Chan Yan Ki & A Chan Fong Ki

runners.name: Happy United
runners.spotlight: Placed over 5f here in March but not at same level lately and remains without a win since January 2024.
runners.comment: Without a win since early 2024 and he looks held by a few of these

runners.name: Beauty Thunder
runners.spotlight: Has a solid record here and was close up behind Grand Nova and Winning Money here three weeks ago, with all three running well from unfavourable draws; stall 6 isn't the end of the world.
runners.comment: Has a solid record here and was close up behind Grand Nova and Winning Money latest

runners.name: Hey Bros
runners.spotlight: Reserve; won in Australia but has yet to get a lot going in Hong Kong and he'd be a surprise winner.
runners.comment: Reserve; won in Australia but has yet to get a lot going in Hong Kong
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Pang Wai Hak, Pang Ka Yeung & Andy Pang Ka Wai

runners.name: Mapogo
runners.spotlight: All three runs at Sha Tin; definite positives to take from the first two (both 5f, turf) and the latest was on dirt (6f), on which he's since won a trial; could feature given he's very lightly raced.
runners.comment: Positives to take from first two runs and has won a trial since latest defeat
runners.owner: Daniel Chong Kwok Ming, Henry Chan Lai Yue & Ng Yim Ping

runners.name: Grand Nova
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; he's up and down and was only midfield at Sha Tin (where he has won) ten days ago, but before then at this track he was just ahead of Winning Money and Beauty Thunder when narrowly missing out from stall 8; if on his game from stall 2 he'd be a danger to all.
runners.comment: Up and down but if on his game from stall 2 he'd be a danger to all

runners.name: Beauty Show
runners.spotlight: All three runs over C&D; similar sort of form in his first two races and for whatever reason he just didn't fire when behind a few of these three weeks ago; he had pushed the winner of that race close on his debut; could be given another chance with Zac Purton again up top.
runners.comment: Failed to fire last time but definite chance on his two previous efforts

runners.name: Bustling City
runners.spotlight: Reserve; went 3-3 in trials before showing improvement on the track with two competitive runs over the 5f at Sha Tin; no match for the winner last time but clearly up to winning at this level.
runners.comment: Reserve; has won trials and competitive in last two races, both over the 5f at Sha Tin

runners.name: Superb Boy
runners.spotlight: Three wins over 5f-7f at Sha Tin, but not in much form lately and he's out in box 12.
runners.comment: Three wins over 5f-7f at Sha Tin, but not in much form lately and he's out in box 12
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Mak Yiu Hay & Mak Kai Chung

runners.name: Harmony Fire
runners.spotlight: Multiple C&D winner who is better drawn now than when behind a handful of these three weeks ago; capable of going well.
runners.comment: Multiple C&D winner who is much drawn now than when behind a handful of these last time

runners.name: Winning Money
runners.spotlight: Defied the outside stall over 6f here in January and, from stall 10, he was only reeled in late on here three weeks ago; good chance off 1lb higher.
runners.comment: From stall 10, he was only reeled in late on three weeks ago; good chance off 1lb higher

runners.name: Grouper
runners.spotlight: Mixed bag in a number of trials and when second on official debut at Sha Tin (6f) he was beaten by an outsider who had been very disappointing in Hong Kong; only 8th back there 17 days ago, again over 6f; drops to 5f from a wicked draw.
runners.comment: Not sure what his second amounts to; drops to 5f from a wicked draw

runners.name: Boltz
runners.spotlight: Made all in a barrier trial in early May but trailed home last in his latest; not sure what to expect on debut with a tongue-tie fitted.
runners.comment: Made all in a barrier trial in early May but trailed home last in his latest

runners.name: Private Wealth
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in one of his seven trials but mostly well held in the others; best watched.
runners.comment: Runner-up in one of his seven trials but mostly well held in the others; best watched

runners.name: Fatal Blow
runners.spotlight: Kept on for fifth over 6f last month and continues to edge down the weights but remains without a win since April 2025 and has stamina to prove on first attempt at 1m.
runners.comment: Last two 6f runs were respectable but he has stamina to prove on first attempt at 1m

runners.name: Viva Firecracker
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after nine runs but has posted two good efforts over C&D since upped in trip in April and continues to edge down the weights; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: 0-9 but has posted two good efforts over C&D since upped in trip in April

runners.name: The Azure
runners.spotlight: Came from off the pace for last-gasp C&D win last month; remains well handicapped on older form and, from stall 12, Zac Purton will presumably drop him in again; respected.
runners.comment: Last-gasp C&D winner last month; still well handicapped on older form; respected
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Alan Ho Pui Lun & Mr & Mrs Norman Tang Fai Ng

runners.name: Sturdy Ruby
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D in October but has not been at his best lately and managed only a midfield finish when dipping back into this grade here last month.
runners.comment: Not at best lately, managing only a midfield finish when back in this grade last month
runners.owner: Adam Chan Yam Man,Brian Wong Chi Fai & Alfred Chan Yam Lung

runners.name: Red Brick Warrior
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all seven starts; made late headway over C&D on last three appearances but his chance may depend upon others setting a strong pace; cheekpieces removed after one use.
runners.comment: Made late headway over C&D on last three outings but is now 0-7; others preferred

runners.name: Blazing Beam
runners.spotlight: Won a Class 5 over 6f here in January but well held in five starts at this level since, most recently when upped to 1m three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Won a Class 5 over 6f here in January but well held in five starts at this level since
runners.owner: Roger Chan Tse Man & Bosco Chan

runners.name: Decision Link
runners.spotlight: Evidently well suited by step up in trip when scoring over C&D three weeks ago and now has 2-12 strike-rate; could still have untapped potential over 1m; high on the list.
runners.comment: C&D winner when upped in trip last month; could still have untapped potential over 1m
runners.owner: Mixion Lam Ting Kwok & Luo Hong Fen

runners.name: Celestial Strider
runners.spotlight: Yet to show anything more than minor promise after five starts (6f-7f) and has awkward draw for first attempt at 1m.
runners.comment: Yet to show anything more than minor promise after five starts (6f-7f)

runners.name: Shooting To Top
runners.spotlight: Reserve; just held on to score over C&D in April, his third start, and was beaten under 1l when fifth behind The Azure here last month; remains lightly raced and will warrant serious consideration if he gets a run.
runners.comment: C&D winner in April, his third run, and finished close up off new mark last month

runners.name: Mighty Steed
runners.spotlight: Reserve; needs to bounce back from a poor performance last month but twice went close over C&D in April and has obvious claims if judged on those efforts.
runners.comment: Ran poorly last month but twice went close over C&D in April; player if back on song

runners.name: General Redwood
runners.spotlight: Improving 4yo who has gained narrow C&D wins under contrasting rides on two of his last three outings; up another 6lb since last time but has better draw today and, after only six runs, is not yet fully exposed.
runners.comment: Improving 4yo who has gained narrow C&D wins on two of his last three outings

runners.name: Absolute Awakened
runners.spotlight: Still 3lb higher than when opening account over C&D in December but did pretty well to finish sixth over C&D 11 weeks ago, having raced on the outer from stall 12; now with a new stable; usual hood/tongue-tie discarded; holds each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: Trapped on outer from stall 12 when sixth over C&D last time; can go well on stable debut

runners.name: Young Bravo
runners.spotlight: Came good with hard-fought 1m1f win here in December, his fourth start; absent since running poorly over 1m3f here three months ago, though, and needs to kick on again to overcome current mark.
runners.comment: Narrow 1m1f winner in December; absent since running poorly over 1m3f here in March

runners.name: Fast Speed
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all six Hong Kong starts; took step in right direction when staying-on fifth at Sha Tin (1m2f) just under a year ago but not seen since and not sure to be suited by this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Best Hong Kong run when staying-on fifth over 1m2f last June; not seen since
runners.owner: Howard Tai Wai Yin & Yim Hin Keung

runners.name: Victor The Rapid
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner; last month's 7f Class 3 defeat can easily be overlooked and his current mark is workable but he's opposable from stall 12; usual tongue-tie left off.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; current mark is workable but outside draw makes this a tough task

runners.name: Lean Master
runners.spotlight: Reserve; has regressed this season but stopped the rot and ran big race from the front when close second over C&D a fortnight ago; remains well handicapped on form as recent as the autumn; interesting to see whether he can build on that recent effort.
runners.comment: Has regressed this season but stopped the rot with a recent C&D second
runners.owner: Cheng Wan Wai, Sheena Cheng Tsz Yan & Selina Cheng Tsz Ching

runners.name: New Power
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner who made pleasing return from six-month break when close third over C&D a fortnight ago; remains on the same workable mark; one to consider.
runners.comment: Back from six-month break with close third over C&D two weeks ago; same mark today
runners.owner: Cheung Tin Chai, Cheung Shu Yan & Cheung Shu Tak

runners.name: Flying Ting Lok
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all nine Hong Kong starts but finished well for fifth over 5f here last month and this move back up in trip looks the right move.
runners.comment: 0-9 in HK but moves back up in trip after finishing well for fifth over 5f last month

runners.name: Live Wire
runners.spotlight: A maiden after six starts but has made the frame three times at Sha Tin (5f-6f) and was beaten under 1l when fifth over C&D last month, having made the running from stall 11; better drawn today and Zac Purton keeps the ride; likely contender.
runners.comment: Beaten under 1l when fifth from stall 11 over C&D last month; Zac Purton rides again

runners.name: Superb King
runners.spotlight: 0-13 in Hong Kong but placed here (5f-6f) on last four appearances, and is well drawn to make another bold bid from the front.
runners.comment: Placed here on last four outings; well drawn to go well from the front again

runners.name: Thunder Prince
runners.spotlight: Belatedly off the mark in Hong Kong with comfortable C&D win in April, his 22nd attempt, but ran poorly here last month and Zac Purton evidently favours Live Wire today.
runners.comment: Belatedly off the mark in HK over C&D in April; deserted by Zac Purton after a poor run
runners.owner: Wilson Chu Kwok Yiu, Paul M F Cheng Et Al

runners.name: Giant Leap
runners.spotlight: Last month's defeat from a wide stall at Sha Tin is not viewed harshly and he remains well handicapped but it's over a year since his last win and he has become inconsistent.
runners.comment: Well handicapped now but it's over a year since his last win and he's become inconsistent

runners.name: Tactical Command
runners.spotlight: Kept on for respectable fifth over C&D two starts ago but has not been at his best this season and was well down the field last time.
runners.comment: Not at best this season and finished well down the field on latest outing

runners.name: Origin Of Form
runners.spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo; unplaced all eight Hong Kong starts and makes very limited appeal from stall 10; hood removed from headgear combination.
runners.comment: Unplaced all eight Hong Kong starts and makes very limited appeal from stall 10

runners.name: Starry Show
runners.spotlight: Yet to finish better than seventh after ten Hong Kong starts and has tough draw today; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Yet to finish better than seventh after ten Hong Kong starts and has tough draw today

runners.name: Warriors Dream
runners.spotlight: Reserve; won readily at Sha Tin in March and ran well there on both starts last month; probably won't be far away if he gets the chance to compete here.
runners.comment: Won readily at Sha Tin in March and ran well there on both starts last month
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Stephen Ho Wai Man, F Ho Ka Chun & D Ho Ka Chung

runners.name: Elegant Life
runners.spotlight: Back in prime form when clearcut C&D winner a fortnight ago and now 2-8 for current stable; 8lb higher today but well drawn again; respected.
runners.comment: Went up 8lb for clearcut C&D win a fortnight ago but has another good draw; respected

runners.name: Escalibor
runners.spotlight: Has the plum inside draw for this official debut but recent trials have been underwhelming; hood/tongue-tie fitted; best watched.
runners.comment: Drawn well for official debut but recent trials have been underwhelming

runners.name: Noisy Boy
runners.spotlight: Kept on for fourth over C&D last month but remains without a win since January 2025 and is patently not the force of old; others have much more appealing profiles.
runners.comment: Kept on for fourth over C&D last month but is patently not the force of old
runners.owner: Leung Lun Ping & Ricky Leung Ghin Ming

runners.name: Le Zonda
runners.spotlight: Yet to be placed after six Hong Kong starts but recent efforts have been respectable and he's slipping down the weights; suited by this C&D; each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: 0-6 in HK but recent efforts have been respectable; each-way claims at least

runners.name: Fortunate Son
runners.spotlight: Gained both wins in Class 4 but has performed well in this higher grade recently, and this move back up in trip from 1m will suit; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Gained both wins in Class 4 but has held his own in this higher grade recently

runners.name: Super Unicorn
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 1m here in early part of this season and returned to form with keeping-on second over today's trip at Sha Tin last month; still on a good mark; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Returned to form with keeping-on second at Sha Tin (1m1f) last month; still on good mark

runners.name: Audacious Pursuit
runners.spotlight: Battled well for narrow 1m course win a fortnight ago and is now 2-12; has more on his plate in this higher grade but the longer trip will suit and he's evidently in good nick.
runners.comment: Battled well for narrow 1m course win; up in grade today; still considered

runners.name: Mission Giant
runners.spotlight: Bettered inauspicious Hong Kong debut and belied huge odds when keeping-on fourth over C&D in April but was well beaten (albeit on soft ground) at Sha Tin (1m2f) last month; not the percentage call.
runners.comment: Fourth at huge odds over C&D in April but well beaten on other to Hong Kong starts

runners.name: A Americ Te Specso
runners.spotlight: Took step back in right direction when fifth over 1m here a fortnight ago and is now 1lb lower than when winning over that trip in December; will be fine over this extra furlong, can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Latest run was a step back in the right direction and he's now 1lb below last winning mark

runners.name: Glittering Legend
runners.spotlight: Went close on dirt two starts ago and is also suited by turf but already has very mixed record after only six runs in Hong Kong; swaps cheekpieces for first-time blinkers; not sure what to expect.
runners.comment: Went close on dirt two starts ago but already has very mixed HK record after only six runs

runners.name: Fantastic Fun
runners.spotlight: Triple 1m winner in Class 4 for new stable this season; probably posted career-best effort when placed in this higher grade in April but wasn't in same form last month and needs to find hidden reserves today.
runners.comment: Three 1m Class 4 wins this season; needs to find hidden reserves in this grade, though
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Hamen Fan Shi Hoo, A Fan Chen Yen & C Fan Chen Mun

runners.name: Shamus Storm
runners.spotlight: 2-13 in Hong Kong, the latter win gained by a short head over this trip at Sha Tin in March; 6lb higher now but has plausible excuses for both subsequent defeats (denied clear run/soft ground) and further progress remains possible.
runners.comment: Won over this trip at Sha Tin in March and has plausible excuses for recent defeats
runners.owner: Lily Chan Li Lei & Marc Chan

runners.name: The Auspicious
runners.spotlight: Three wins this season, the latest when leading close home over C&D in April; also finished well when second over C&D last month and can make his presence felt again.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D in April and also finished well when second over C&D last month

runners.name: Loch Tay
runners.spotlight: Reserve; dual 1m dirt winner at Sha Tin this season; made the frame off today's mark over that C&D last month but needs to prove he can match that form on turf.
runners.comment: Dual dirt winner this season; needs to prove he can hit the same heights on turf

runners.name: Fallon
runners.spotlight: Looked as good as ever when scoring over C&D in March but recent efforts have been disappointing and he's still on a tough mark.
runners.comment: Won over C&D in March but recent efforts have been disappointing

runners.name: Refusetobeenglish
runners.spotlight: Looked unlucky not to finish closer than fifth at Sha Tin (6f; denied clear run) in March but disappointed over 7f there the following month and is now 0-10 in Hong Kong.
runners.comment: Below form at Sha Tin (7f) in April and now 0-10 in Hong Kong
runners.owner: 24/25 Pierre Ng Pang Chi Trainer Syndicate

runners.name: Chateauneuf
runners.spotlight: Suffered prolonged slump in form after winning over C&D in November but was seemingly back in good nick when fourth here three weeks ago; well drawn again today and remains on a good mark; player if able to build on that recent effort.
runners.comment: Seemed to have emerged from spell in the doldrums when fourth here last month

runners.name: Friends Of Shajing
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 4yo; 0-7 in Hong Kong for Danny Shum but has made the frame three times over C&D this season; makes stable debut after 105-day break; considered each-way.
runners.comment: 0-7 in Hong Kong but has made the frame three times over C&D this season; stable debut

runners.name: Gummy Gummy
runners.spotlight: Gained both Hong Kong wins on dirt but has been placed twice over C&D since returned to turf in April, losing out only by a short head a fortnight ago; not ideally drawn today but still enters calculations.
runners.comment: Placed twice over C&D since returned to turf in April and enters calculations
runners.owner: Alan Kiang Ping Fai & Joyce Lee

runners.name: Spicy Gold
runners.spotlight: Well down the field on first two starts this season but his close third over C&D a fortnight ago was considerably better, and he remains well handicapped on older form.
runners.comment: Well beaten on first two stable starts but his recent close third here was much better

runners.name: All Out For Six
runners.spotlight: Smart when based in South Africa and not beaten far on any of first four Hong Kong starts (albeit without being placed); dropped another 2lb since last time; a possible.
runners.comment: Smart when based in South Africa and has made pretty solid start to Hong Kong career
runners.owner: James Lau Po Man & Alice Woo Wai See

runners.name: Master Champion
runners.spotlight: Reserve; made fairy respectable stable debut over 6f last month but finished last over 5f a few days later and has become hard to predict.
runners.comment: Ran okay on last month's stable debut but well beaten since; has become hard to predict

runners.name: Telecom Fighters
runners.spotlight: Belied triple-digit odds when fourth from stall 11 over C&D last month but was quickly back in the doldrums here a week later.
runners.comment: Fourth at triple-digit odds here last month but quickly back in the doldrums a week later

runners.name: Dancing Classics
runners.spotlight: Reserve; low-mileage 5yo who won twice in Class 4 last year (5f/6f); returned from layoff with low-key run last week, though, and still needs to prove himself in this grade.
runners.comment: Dual Class 4 winner last year; returned from break with low-key run last week

runners.name: Aurio
runners.spotlight: Progressive for new stable this season, winning twice (once over C&D) and making the frame on his other six appearances; Zac Purton keeps the ride but outside draw an obvious negative today.
runners.comment: Progressive and consistent for new stable this season but has tough draw today

runners.name: Tycoon Resources
runners.spotlight: Launched career with two 5f course wins, getting up close home on both occasions; moves up in grade today but is well drawn and should have more to offer over the extra furlong.
runners.comment: Launched career with two narrow 5f course wins; should have more to offer over 6f

runners.name: Greater Bae
runners.spotlight: Won in pretty good style over C&D in January and remains lightly raced in Hong Kong but didn't fire last time and has awkward draw today; needs a good boost from the new blinkers.
runners.comment: Didn't fire last time and has awkward draw today; needs good boost from new blinkers

runners.name: Shanghai Warrior
runners.spotlight: Made winning debut in 5.5f maiden in Australia in January; remains an unknown quantity but has awkward draw for Hong Kong debut.
runners.comment: Made winning debut in 5.5f maiden in Australia in January; now makes Hong Kong debut

runners.name: Szeryng
runners.spotlight: Made good progress last year, winning twice at Sha Tin; unraced since running as though something was amiss there in January but has won a couple of recent trials and has the plum inside draw for this comeback.
runners.comment: Progressive in 2025; unraced since January but has won two recent trials and is well drawn
runners.owner: Dr Edwin Lam Chi Wan & Jason Lam Che Man
